reviews.text:
Wonderful App. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I absolutely love using the Athena Patient app! It has made keeping up with my doctor appointments and test results so much easier and less stressful. I can quickly check upcoming appointments, view my test results, and stay informed about my healthcare without having to make multiple phone calls.

The app is easy to navigate, and I appreciate having all of my important health information right at my fingertips. It helps me feel more involved in my healthcare journey and gives me peace of mind knowing I can access my information anytime I need it.

I am very thankful for this app and highly recommend it to anyone who wants a convenient and reliable way to stay connected with their healthcare providers

Horrible. This app is absolutely horrible and frustrating. Why have an app that I have to log into every single time and it routes you to a website within the app it self… it’s like being in the 90s. Additionally you request a password word reset and it take 10min for the email to show up. Worst app ever
Lack patient information. There should be a tab where patient can access their weight, height etc
Agenda. Me parece muy buena idea de llevar un Porfolio de las citas,laboratorios y de los medicamentos
diabetes diagnosis was well supported. easy to chat with the nurses and I easily got answers within 24 hours. Glad this app existed!

Multiple mix ups. Loved the doctors, they genuinely seem to care. My issue is the first four or five times I was seen it was by the same provider. Randomly one of my appointments got canceled. That provider had emailed me previously so I was able to get in touch with her by email to get something set up that same day because I was running out of my medication. And the app was telling me her next available was a week out. The doctor told me my appointment was canceled because she was double booked and the app canceled it which I thought was crazy. So I go to my next scheduled appointment and it’s with a different provider. I get a message in the portal that my doctor is no longer with them. Which I didn’t even see that message until the day of my appointment. OK great so I have been seeing that doctor for about 3 to 4 months. And I had dropped my local doctor. Well the new doctor tells me per my insurance. I have to have a mandatory hour long session in the middle of the day weekly. Um ok? I work a full-time 9 to 5 job. That’s not possible.  The doctor was kind enough to write my script for the month and told me she was sorry. So I reached out to the app and say well let me just be self pay and they said no. So initially, why was this not told to me by my first provider? Why would they not give the option for people to be self-pay especially when the payment is collected before the scheduled visit? They are responsive to messages and like I said the providers do seem to genuinely care. But behind the scenes, it seems like they don’t have it together
Portal is a big downgrade from previous. very difficult to navigate.  Did not port over previous patient information.  Can’t schedule easily on-line.  Need serious improvement
Kathleen Dalton. Kathleen Dalton is a fabulous doctor or nurse practitioner in the psychiatric department. She listens and she really tries to help me. I recommend her
My helper. The Porter is a good app for a person that’s getting old and can’t remember too good
Visit notes. I would like to be able to see my visit notes
Rescheduling. Love the fact it’s easy to go in and just do that very convenient

Not able to access to my health records. This app keeps logging me out ; not sure what’s the glitch even after resetting my password  I’m not able to login into my health chart neither does this portal information synced with MyChart. Such a useless health app

Garbage. Absolutely awful. Messages don’t come through, app shows no record of appointments that are confirmed. I have to keep calling my doctor for information that I should able to access in the app. Makes me want to switch doctors just to avoid using this horrible app
Great app!!. It’s a great app! Easy to get in, get what you need done, and get out. Easy to access!!❤️

Exam & Follow-up. Dr. Cabrera was thorough and explained previous tests to me. She was kind and compassionate- I wish I lived closer to her office.
Dr. Cabrera’s staff was friendly and helpful.
Thank you!
I love it. I no longer have to deal with the nasty receptionist attitudes at my doctor office
App. I love the app it is really great to keep up with results and medication and chat with ur doctors
Very helpful. Amazing for communication!

“Very Friendly”. I’ve been going there for years, and I’ve never had one bad day that I can complain about. I started with Dr Jay I think in the late 70’s. Everyone has been great!! That is why I still return…
Mintier. Good
Not Impressed. Attempting to log in via the app and all I get is a white blank screen. Uninstalled and reinstalled with the same results, I’m unable to log in

Doctor. So easy to access and makes communication with your provider fast and simple!
Horrible. Just signed up for an account and the app and web site don’t recognize my credentials. Clicked on the forgot password link and never sent me a reset kink

Such quick response responses. First, the app is so easy to use. And secondly, I have never had such quick responses. I recently had a concern about a surgery incision took a picture sent it in and was interacting online with the doctor within an hour. Just amazing!
Great. I’m new at using it so will be learning as i go

Excellent Experience with Dr. Ashley.. I had a wonderful experience with Dr. Ashley and her entire office today. Dr. Ashley is absolutely fantastic — kind, professional, attentive, and very caring. The nurse who took my blood pressure and weight was also very friendly and made me feel comfortable right away.

The front desk staff were welcoming and helpful, and the check-in process was quick and easy. Everyone in the office was professional and pleasant. I truly appreciate the excellent care and positive experience. Highly recommend!
Best care ever. My NP Angie Brown is amazing!   She’s become family

Awesome app!. I just love the way that I got a notification on my appointments. And the ease of use. I found out found out found out that I have appointments that I that I didn’t realize that I had. So I need to call and cancel them. Thanks for a wonderful app!
Service. Great
Easy to use. Love the new patient portal it’s easy to use and it has all the important information all organized like for confirming appointments / check in

Communication. I feel this allows me to see results to my test as well as any instruction she may have for me. Being able to ask questions and getting a reply quickly allows me to either get an appointment or follow any direction she may have for me. Medication refills is such a nuisance and I feel through the Athena portal, it keeps me from feeling like a bother

Horrible app. Worst medical app I’ve ever used. Totally inadequate for information. It doesn’t provide access to the patient portal. You have to do a Google search to get there. Athena is poorly designed, not user friendly and lousy at providing information. Too bad Northwest is so cheap that it doesn’t provide a useful EMRfor its patients. EPIC is the bomb. Funny how El Rio Health uses it but banner and northwest are both too cheap to invest in a superior product

Clearly the developers are keeping busy and for that I am grateful. I now enjoy using the app and regularly access it. Bravo Springfield Clinic!

My issues with the app are on the functionality of the app, the app has a Face ID option; however, it is never used. My doctor was at Complete Health in Alabama, that shows up - but for some reason the main Complete Health in Florida also shows up, I’ve never used the Florida location and my doctor has left Complete Health and created his own practice - so now I have three practices and only one valid one. 

Now, once I get to his practice, the app works well. It’s the having to sign-in every time, lack of face id even though the app has an option for this, and having to walk through two pages of - here are the practices you are associated with click continue, and I can’t remove or change any of them, is frustrating and makes me not want to use the app

The Athena app has also been a huge help. It keeps all my information in one place—appointments, messages, visit summaries, and results—making communication and follow‑up so much easier. It really improves the whole experience and helps me stay on top of my care.

Highly recommend Dr. Schindler, his team, and the Athena app for anyone looking for organized, efficient, and patient‑focused care

Simply fantastic Work. Hi, my name is JOREL I am a 47 year young blind person. I have been going blind since birth and was diagnosed at the age of eight. And have never let this limitation. Overcome my goals. Although. Without the certain low vision aids or technology that I did not have while growing up. I have never been able to read File manage my own personal documents This day now is a milestone With the help that Apple provides with their accessibility settings and now the accessibility team that works building the platform at Athena healt I have been given the option andh freedom with a sense of an empowerment and independence i’ve always been a strong learner, dedicated and driven to overcome my visual impairment but when this happens, not just, I but confidently speaking for the whole of the world as it would be coming from a blind or visually impaired person this very software used at Athena health is groundbreaking it gives a sense of hope to those that may have none a link where the chain was broken and independence absolutely words cannot express the gratitude I feel knowing that this is just the beginning for accessibility and a new beginning for those who may have lost their way thank you so much for making my life a lot easier
Sincerely

My doctor & nurse. Hi, I just want to say how thankful I am that Dr.Laurie was brought in my life many many years ago. From the beginning of her first prescribing Suboxone. Great but needs some improvement. Overall I enjoy the ease of being able to catch up on charting, quickly pull up patient information, and check my inbox without having to lug my computer around (especially when on call); however, there are a few things that could make the app better. 

The first thing that could be improved would be being able to add patient problems, allergies, histories, vital signs, address/phone numbers, etc. as I’m reviewing charts instead of having to log onto the computer later on to do so. Another thing would be being able to complete billing from the app so that when I finish my charting and close the encounter I don’t have to remember to go back and bill for it. The last thing I can think to be added would be having the ability to take pictures of rashes, bruises, etc. in the athenaOne app instead of going through multiple apps to upload one in real time during an appointment. 

If possible, having a dedicated patient portal app would be wonderful so that patients can more easily send messages, upload/update information, and view their medical records

Does not update patient status appropriately. Colored banners do not update to show status of patients (ie checked in, ready for provider). I can use this app for everything else, but knowing when a patient arrives and is ready is pretty important

No access to patient phone contact numbers.. This app is pretty useless. If I’m away from the office, I can view my schedule.  However if I need to contact a patient this app will not provide a simple phone number to reach the patient. The old app provided that information and more with no problem . Why would you change a good thing and  create problems when it comes to patient servicing? ......and now the mobile app will not allow me to sign in with my user name and password

Almost Useless. If I’m lucky enough to not get stuck in an endless login loop: “we see it’s the first time you’ve been logging in!” I can sometimes see my schedule. Evil app. Athena and athenaOne are EXACTLY what an EMR should NOT be.
I have never seen an EMR that cannot trend labs… ( how can you trend a pdf scan?). I am a nephrologist that works on trends…
AthenaOne’s other non starter issue with me is that it messes completely the schedule of the desktop version that i have to rearrange every time i make the mistake of opening athena one. They used to have another mobile app, which despite being clunky like all of Athena, at least didn’t mess my schedule.
In summary, this is an evil application that is one of the main reasons i am considering early retirement

Can’t search documents. The new app is not nearly as personal as the old one it is quicker in some respects but you can’t search old documents at all with any significant date range and for example if you’re trying to search of patients operative report history you can’t go more than a few months back or any window more than a few months. It’s really hampers the ability to search old records on a patient and the old version of the app did not have this problem. I’ve asked multiple times to have this fixed from the software engineers to no avail

AthenaOne. This app is nice for seeing the upcoming schedule, but I am less impressed with not seeing the options to address things in my bucket that I see while I am at work on the laptop. Also, it seems I have apply my preferences for the bucket each time I log on

Horrible replacement app. The current app is simply unusable 
Very clunky 
Multiple touches to get even the most simple things done. 
PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE RETURN TO OLD PLATFORM

AthenaOne. The old Chrome app to get into Athena was MUCH BETTER. Don’t be surprised if Unified or Athena starts getting complaints about Providers not responding quickly when out of the office. Even signing onto AthenaOne is a big issue
Horrible. This is the worst app ever. You cannot schedule patients. You cannot properly do intakes. It’s horrible

Not sure if anyone vetted this app. Ever since being forced to change to this app for mobile devices. There has been several issues which no one seems to even try to correct. 
Forced closes often needed to get Face ID to work. 
Cannot close items in inboxes as easily as prior app.

The prep feature is very clunky and essentially a waste bc you can’t toggle through labs and the note together.

Signing orders is painful. Signing orders is not as quick and efficient as it is within the browser application and therefore takes much longer to complete.  Inefficient use of screen space.  Getting from one order to the next is not smooth.  Could be done a lot better as I know AthenaHealth is capable
Doesn’t work overseas on vacation. Can’t get caught up on work while overseas.  App doesn’t work in foreign countries

Great ideas but need work. This app is a great idea but does need some work to make it more efficient and effective. For example, when using an iPad with an external keyboard, AthenaText will not allow for the conversation to be shown horizontally which requires me to type sideways. Also, I am not able to pull information from the last encounter note into the open encounter note as you are able to do through the website and I have not found the option to amend previously closed encounters so I am having to wait to do that until I get home to my physical laptop. Please give us the web browser back. We are your customers.  Make the app more physician friendly.  Impossible to navigate a d missing crucial data such as procedure notes

Athena one mobile app. Do not like it at all

Very slow, but otherwise good. Interface is very slow and takes a while to login

Awful. As a doctor, time and ease of use are so important. This app does not take that into account. The web app was perfect. It was simple, and you got to the necessary information in the fewest clicks possible. The web app is no longer supported and the athenaOne app is a huge step down. What used to be three clicks is now 15. There is so much unnecessary information on the screen. It is much more difficult to find the information that I need

Needs considerable update before sunsetting Athena mobile. Half the functionality of Athena Out of office. For example, when looking at CBC need to scroll through 5+ screens to get to previous one for comparison

Too many flaws and glitches. Unfortunately I cannot use this app reliably right now. There are so many glitches and moments where the app freezes. It’s much more of a hassle than helpful right now. Currently using on up to date iPhone 12 with most recently updated Athena One app

Awful. Why can’t athenaOne use normal tech functions like reply, close, send.  Just Trying to reply to a patient case (message) means filtering through tons of jargon garbage. All overly complicated and not designed for clinicians.  Designed for software developers

Mobile app. You can only look up some information on the app and pre-chart. You can not actually chart on the app. I have a house call business so this makes it impossible to chart real time with this app

Can you please make an app for patients?. Hello,

I love using the Athena health patient portal. However, I feel as if the patient portal would be more efficient if it were an app. The portal has similar user interface and experience design to an app
Works poorly. Freezes up   Poorly executed app. Rarely use. I use a iPhone 12 Pro Max

Click happy. Death by a thousand clicks. 2-3 times as long to perform same tasks as required in some competitors’ EMR design.  Fairly disorganized and discombobulated data presentation and almost non existent filters to permit finding targeted or user- specific notes and data over extended periods. 

The technology exists to design an EMR that is intuitive, learns users patterns and permits user- specific work flows. One day there will be an EMR that physicians truly want to use rather than are compelled to use.  For now, we’ll have to be content spending more time focusing on data entry and clicking through bureaucratic hoops over actually focusing on patient care.   How did physicians loose control of our work environment ?

New iPhone update incompatibility. Recently updated my iphone to version 14.4.2 and I am unable to login to the AthenaOne app. When I enter my username and click continue, the app will spin for a brief moment and then the screen does not advance. Frustrating, especially with the upcoming disabling of mobile browser access!

Great for adding photos, would like better writing support. This app is really wonderful for being able to quickly log into your patient list for the day. It is also very convenient for adding photos, for example of skin lesions, to a patient’s chart. No synchronizing your phone to a patient’s chart or anything like you have to with “Athena capture”. 

There are a couple of issues I would love to see addressed. The first is that when you’re viewing the schedule, the app automatically right refreshes every so often which jumps you back to the top of the schedule making it very frustrating, if not impossible to follow along during a team huddle. The second thing is that I would love to see write to text support in the text fields. This only makes sense for a mobile app that many providers will use on an iPad to help quickly document things in a room

Head spinning. Why did you phase out the old app? The new app, in my humble opinion, is horribly designed and frustrating. I can’t get to old encounter documents without going through an endless number of clicks. I prep by looking up my old encounter and reviewing old encounter documents (audiograms, scope pictures). Now it takes me 10-15 min to just find what I need to review(after practicing for an hour!) and that doesn’t even count reviewing! Please don’t delete the old app it was easy to use and I don’t need all this extraneous information just for a quick review.
I’m sure the prep part will be good but for those of us who use it to review the patients for the upcoming day this app is a nightmare. 
Are physicians involved in your designs??

Poor app functionality. I have my own private practice and it is just me for all the roles. I LIVE with my cell phone app to see my schedule to add and reschedule patients all day long. I cannot do either with this app. I can access my schedule but I cannot modify it. PRACTICALLY USELESS. It is also very difficult to see my schedule and change each week to access my availability. Frustrating app which I wish I didn’t HAVE to use every day

Love this app. This app is wonderful and extremely useful. I love the biometric security that enables the Face ID. The only thing I would suggest adding is being able to also use a 4 digit pin. Often times when we are in the office we have a mask on and I have to remove it or type out my full password in order to access the app. It would be helpful that if I have a mask on the Face ID automatically transitions into asking for a pin. Other than that I love the app and it is functioning flawlessly

Amazing. It was nice to have to old one to look at basic info, if needed, but now, I have the same accessibility as I would on a laptop or computer. I also love being able to access Athena text in the same app
AthenaOne Health Portal app. The Username login doesn’t work. Tried 2 login with my gmail account and it won’t take it all. Please Help!!!

“Any member of your practice”...?. I manage a medical practice and was excited to hear that this app would allow members of the practice to log in and use Athena. While this is the EMR used by our practice but I am
unable to log in. After a week of inquiries and being told several times the error was on our end (my profile needed to be “changed” to allow me access), I was told it was because my organization want included in the “any member” release. Disappointed

Great improvement. This app being available to staff is a huge help for when the Internet is out. The only improvement I would suggest is to be able to check the patient in somehow! We had clinic hours recently during an internet outage and we were able to do a lot from the phone app except for checking in

This app helps a LOT. This app was badly needed and it is pretty useful. I use it when I get questions about patients or when rounding in the hospital.  Athena will surely make adjustments to the app as time goes by making it even more useful

When it works, not intuitive. I’ve tried this app on a number of occasions. It works maybe 70% of the time, and once I’m on I can rarely figure out how to get the information I need. I don’t have time to learn how to use an app. It should work and be intuitive enough to figure out on the fly, like almost all other apps

Helps watch things when out of office. I find this app very useful for checking labs, rads and consults quickly from wherever I happen to be. New new notification of Athena text is very helpful (sometimes not looking at desk top and sometimes the Athena text function quite working on the laptop and there was no way to know without checking - now there is! Could use work on reliably opening with faceID (it unlocks but says “credentials expired, log in again - which I do but still need to force close app and do it again for app to open). Finally it is now easier to send patient feedback on labs and rads, but it is still hard to tell if PT has portal access;  all-in-all it saves time and improves access - mainly when away from my laptop
Could be great but it’s not...... It would be nice if you could input patient vitals but I can’t seem to find out how

Portal for patients. This app would be much more popular if you created and android/iOS app in conjunction with the physicians app. Accessing your patient portal online is difficult and extremely slow! Please work on this

Great app for providers! Much better than the old one.. Love that I can use my iPad for my encounters. Few suggestion  that can improve my daily routine .

1. Faster loading and refresh times 
2. I wish that when I open up to document within any section (ie. Free Text), that it can just open a bubble or a smaller window, as oppose to a whole window that takes up the whole screen. 
3. A smaller front page to display the inbox and messages and more emphasis on the patient schedules. Similar to he desktop version. 
4. In regards to replying/closing labs and documents, I would be nicer to have the action placed nexts to the text box.
5. Give us he ability to determine text sizes on the screen. The current text size is too big. 
6. Lastly, gotta have a dark mode setting !

Thank you for creating this app!
Patient. How do I cancel this and credit my account. I’m not a provider
It would be great if it worked on my iPhone.. This app seems to be unable to go back to select a different patient of to plain look again the the scheduled appointments. It just freezes. A shame

Superb. Simply the easiest and most complete EHR to begin with, athenahealth has produced a completely usable app which allows providers to do anything and everything right from a phone or an iPad. Even seeing patients and documentation is easy and dictation is absolutely perfect. 
Cannot do better. The best. Full. Stop

It’s not great. And the tech support is garbage.. The app itself is a little clunky and tedious to navigate at times. However, the real downfall of Athena is their support team. It’s absolute trash. If you can’t fix the issue yourself, prepare to have weeks pass before your issue is resolved or better yet, acknowledged as a verified issue and then subsequently informed that they won’t do anything about it until more people complain about a similar issue

MD. The main reason why this application is not a five out of five rating is because they have failed to include a built-in dictation (speech to text) modality within the app.  Otherwise, it is an extremely useful application, with the best component being the patient prep feature that allows you to populate the chart prior to the patient visit, thus giving you more time to focus on the patient and not in charting
Physician. Very useful and functional app. Definitely helps ease the flow of my day whether at work or home
Great App. This app has changed my life. The prepping has given me my life back and I’m no longer charting all night and working all day. I’ve never had a problem. I love it

Crash. The app has great potential, but falls short due to instability.  It keeps locking up or crashing for me.  I’m not sure that it is a cost effective use of my time.  Spending hours outside of the clinic to save minutes during my clinical time is at best frustrating.  

I am not sure if they are addressing the frequent system crashes, but if they could make the app more stable, it would be well worth having it.  As it is now I only use it when I have ample “down time” to allow for the time sink of using the app

The best mobile EMR. This has replaced my dictation software and my need for a large electronic device like a laptop. I can chart prep from anywhere at anytime. Thanks Athena

B+ grade. This Athena app is convenient but it does take some time to figure out how it works as opposed to the desk top version. I haven’t been able to determine how to sign off/close notes but that has so far been my only real issue. I’m looking forward to the updates!
Del Río NP. Great application, I’m very busy between ER and outpatient practice, This app makes easy yo place order and finish my notes. I can even dictate using my microphone

Frustrating complications. I gotta say, this app/service in general left me more frustrated than anything else. It worked perfectly sending me a text to verify the account as it should until the medical provider part. After that i could not get it to text (the number it literally had just texted) me at all, prompting me to retry on three separate internet browsers and eventually the app to get it working. Still wouldn't. So I reset the whole account, set up a security question instead and wow, "the Athena health service is unable to log you in!" Error came up. OK.... one last thing, I'll call the people with the app then. After talking with the lady on the phone, she told me that they can't access the medical records of patients, ok that's fine, I don't want you to anyways I just want to be able to use the app. So then she tells me that it must be something with the medical provider not having provided my number? Even though I got an invite from them and it all worked up until the point that the app needed to text me a second time? Maybe it is something with them but now I'm just confused and upset

Brings much needed capabilities. I’m THRILLED at the ability to prep encounter notes ahead of visits, saving a lot of in-clinic time. 
This app seems to have a lot of great capabilities, even if the workflow is significantly different than the desktop site. 

The major flaw (or is it me?) is that I can document an entire HPI, exam, orders, etc, but I cannot figure out how to finish billing and close an encounter?? Bummer to do the whole thing on the iPad, and then have to log into a computer to close them.

Looking forward to continued improvements!

Not Meant for Smartphones. Disappointed:

The view is cluttered. So hard to view information in a streamlined fashion

The new version does seem to upload attachments - usually images - faster and more reliably than the old one did

Love It. I Love Love Love Athena text because its so easy and convenient. I can send and receive messages on my cell phone and don't have to worry about pulling out my laptop or getting on a desk top computer. Thank you so much for this app!

Versatile. I can’t believe it allows me to do so much on my phone . Easy, practical and very effective. All the beauty of Athena in the palm of your hand! The updates improve flow in ways I didn’t think possible!

Finally working. After about 3 to 4 weeks of it not working well I deleted the app and reinstalled it a few times, it worked.I like that it is a good way to keep in touch with my coworkers while I’m not in the office and I can get to the messages pretty quickly

AthenaText saves me!. The athenaText app saved me time in so many ways! Communicating with staff for add-ons, cancellations, changes in schedule, communication with colleagues and improving workflow

Love this. I absolutely love Athena text it keeps me up to date when I’m not near my computer or at work. It is great I love it

Great software!!. Communication in healthcare is instrumental to meeting any organizational Mission. AthenaText helps healthcare teams communicate with efficiency and accuracy. Improving communication. Great way to securely communicate with people in group

Incredible app. The Athena messenger app is incredible. It allows me to communicate quickly and efficiency with my clinic staff under HIPPA compliance. Still at work in progress, but doing much better. I think it’s very functional and works quite well. My one issue is getting alerts. I have to receive a message from my staff telling me to check my app as it does not pop up notifications for me to let me know that I have a message
Great part my Athena experience. Great

App needs work. The concept of this app is great but it needs work. Every time I make a comment about it, I’m told to remove the app and redownload it. No other app do I have to do that with. Great app!!. I like the fact that even when I’m away from work I can still receive messages sent to my phone

Love Love Athena Text. We do great communication with Athena Text. This is the one of the BEST feature in AthenaNET. Terrible coding. As a clinician in a high-volume setting, I depend on a mobile EHR that is fast, stable, and memory-efficient. NextGen Mobile continues to suffer from major performance issues that appear rooted in unaddressed memory leaks, poor lifecycle management, and inefficient background thread handling. The app routinely hangs during asynchronous data calls, fails to release system resources properly, and crashes without error reporting or logging that’s visible to the end user, making it impossible to give real app feedback to the dev team. 

I’ve submitted tickets consistently, yet the core problems persist across updates and devices. The app’s failure to handle caching, session management, and multithreaded operations makes it ridiculously unreliable in a setting where efficiency is absolutely critical.

This is not a UI complaint or isolated bug. These are systemic performance architecture issues that need to be prioritized by the development team. Clinicians are depending on this app in real time, and the current level of instability is unacceptable

Makes me feel like a doctor again. I really like this app. I have used Dragon Medical with my EHR in the past, but I feel that this is better. 

Being able to dictate the visit into your smart phone changes the way that you see the patient. Even though I still have a computer with the EHR in the exam room, I no longer need to use it for data input, which means I no longer feel like a glorified data clerk.   Instead, I can use the computer/EHR to find information, share it with the patient if needed, and then summarize my thoughts and do all the charting immediately after the visit when I dictate into my iPhone.  

It also has the advantage of giving me 24/7 access to my schedule and to patient charts.  It also makes it easy to snap a photo of a clinical finding during the encounter and add it to the patient’s chart.   And I can securely message other providers using the app.    I haven’t used the remote scribe feature

eClinicalMobile. “ Like father like son.” 

eCW has done it again. Daddy has a total lack of any logic, ditto for sonny. 

Why is it that we can not simply answer a phone and press “send?” What sense does it make to use a “ save” button to send a message? 

It is hard to believe that this company has managed to cook so many so many flaws into their software that they claim to be unable to fix.
A couple of examples:
  
1. You can not use a simple hyphen! We use this little piece of punctuation repeatedly during the course of writing/dictating notes on a daily basis. This is an absolute absurdity! 
2. When filling out a prescription on “ Big Daddy”  and entering  the # , the quantity disappears at last half the time. What kind of bone headed screw up is that !? Absolute nonsense!
3. Why are there at least 3 different medication lists and yet only one of those can be used to manage the medications?

Did eCW even remotely consider having endusers try this unwieldy excuse for a piece of equipment that is supposed to make our lives more streamlined and efficient. My recommendation is that  eCW Pay those of us that were duped into purchasing this misery generating excuse for an EHR

Unreliable. It’s great to have the features that it offers, but what good does it do IF IT DOESNT WORK THE MAJORITY OF TIME?!?  I’ve tried to point this out to our EClinical rep over and over, and all they do is make updates to “fix the bugs”.  These inevitably create other “bugs”.  The truth behind eClinicals in general, not just the mobile app, is that whatever program base they have is simply flawed from the beginning. It’s horribly programmed.  If you could visualize the program represented by a tangible structure, I see it as a Dr Seuss drawing of a complex scaffolding that is thrown together and held together by mismatched materials and temporary fragile repairs. Anything and everything will and does go wrong with it.  Perhaps when you signed up with them you were fooled by the nice format of the notes. It gives the misperception that it must be a great EHR. But the very first thing any EHR has to be is RELIABLE, otherwise none of the fancy features account for anything.  The EClinical mobile app does not perform reliably enough to even allow you to use it, so you might as well not even have it
App freezes a lot. Especially when reviewing labs. Fix the bugs! Update. 9/2021. Has been months and this bug is still not fixed

This mobile app works well and is a big help on call to review diagnosis, lab history and make a new phone encounter. I reviewed labs waiting at the dentist yesterday.  

There is a lot of pressure to switch to Epic from large health systems - but ECW continues to compete for our business as independent practices.  This Ap is a good enhancement

Modest improvements. I use it primarily to check daily schedule, to e-prescibe Meds/refills (unreliable), and to document text messages (allows copy and paste into a new telephone encounter)

Not at all user friendly. Cumbersome to navigate. Seemingly o option to send new prescriptions in a telephone encounter. I gave up on this and generally have to use a computer to do anything but the most rudimentary function

Great for photo capture. The app gives you a chance to look at your schedule, read messages, etc but is pretty limited beyond that. It does a slick job of taking pics off the iPhone and putting them into the patient chart quickly
Buggy and slow. This app should have been a lot better by now than what it is. Given how expensive EHR licenses are, it is shameful how limited and painfully slow this app is. Disappointing
Disappointing. Has not met expectations or promises made more than two years ago
Good app so that we don't have to open if screen. Needs more info to make us completely computer free

App is more user-friendly than the computer program. Certain parts of this app or easier to use than the computer program itself. For instance eprescription refills can be filled with a single click of a button. 

It does need to be expanded though. There are definitely parts of the chart they are unable to be viewed.

It's a good start, but could use more functionality
Review. Good app for basic things only.  Its limitations should be clearly stated
Ok. Not the best, but it serves the purpose
Does the job. Wish this was better but does the job. I continuously switch between the desktop and the mobile version. In tandem works better
Very limited function. I use it because I have to.  Fairly buggy.  Too many functions are missing
Mediocre at best. The app is only useful for contact info look up. Everything else is not user friendly. Takes forever to load info

Meh. Works but not super functional. Does not seem to let me fax from mobile app (allows me to go through the motions, but faxes don't go through). 
Difficult to navigate- and impossible to document from device. Schedule view is difficult to understand

Rudimentary, needs more functionality. I find that this app falls short on many levels. I cannot see scanned documents, and cannot scribe into all of the sections of the chart. I cannot document directly from the encounter view- it should be hyperlinked so I can add whatever is necessary to any section of the note. At times the dictations do not get scribed into the chart- it says 'timed out'. Coding for the visit is next to impossible. It is frustrating that I cannot see the body of the telephone encounter. I do like that I can answer some labs on the fly. Please make app more functional

Good but needs work. It works well for basically 'read only' situations. It is difficult to make/write notes and do billing. If it could be a little more like the normal ECW, it would be nice. Also, why can't it have the same passwords as ECW office version?  Inconvenient

Needs work. From physician side limited number of people I can turn a telephone encounter over to. Not able to view documents. Every time it updates it loses all of the sign in information , usually when I'm traveling and don't have easy access to chart. I do love that I can answer questions on the fly and put photos of lesions directly in the Pt's chart
Very poor app.. Unbelievable that they have such a lousy app

Incredibly frustrating. One of the most frustrating apps to use ever. Loves to lock you out, ask you for information you can't possibly know and then require you to go to a desktop to have a prayer to fix it. The phone service is miserably slow and takes days to get answers. Not a mobile application

App is of no use. App will only show data from 2012 which does me no good and Ecw support is a joke even if you could understand them they read from a Manual and never seem to help but they always have an excuse for why the app can't do what it is suppose to do
Pt list. Patient list / schedule does not update automatically. For the amount payed for this EMR app would be outstanding in performance
App hangs. App hangs and times out when trying to bring up notes
Helpful, but needs something more. It's a nice app, but you need to be able to access the docs part of the chart.  Please add this functionality
Frustrated Practitioner. Worst app ever!
Poor app. Cannot review progress notes and tech support is not helpful at all
Getting better. Improved with the recent updates but would like to be able to access documents.  It is convenient to pull up notes on the run

Very Nice Update. Very, very helpful App when on the go. 

Some functions, like refills, are actually more convenient than using my "E" jellybean at the office. 

Clearly eCW remains on the cutting edge and remains dedicated to continual improvement in this fast paced and ever evolving electronic world
Pretty useful. Not fully functional but a great resource to quickly access the system

Get rid of the daily User Log Reports.. Or, at least, allow us to turn off the notifications. It is extremely annoying to be woken up by these pointless alerts everyday. I don't need an alert to tell me I haven't used the app in the past 24 hrs. Why would anyone need that?!?!

Not too helpful. 5 providers were kicked out and needed to reinstall after the last update. 2 needed to reset passwords in the EMR to get back in. An eCW tech from the NEQCA forum was extremely unhelpful and rude to one of our partners who was having trouble. 

Aside from that, the app offers little use since many visits don't show up, sync is slow, and it crashes occasionally.
We basically only use it to take pictures in the office of a patient's rash or injury. It's a one-trick pony at this point. 

Please develop this app
Scribe. Thank you for adding scribe to the app. Awesome!!

Attn!!. Writing this review in hopes it gets fixed quickly, I downloaded this app on my iPad 3 mini and found that it deactivates my front facing camera. After deleting the app it works again. Please fix. Thank you!

Impressive. Excellent app with serious potential. Probably not that great as a standalone, so I wonder if some of the negative comments are from users who don't really understand its purpose. It's a very powerful add on pocket app to eCW with very useful functionality. Not intended as a full EMR. The iPad touch version does that and is outstanding. As an Ob would be great if it could keep a running list of patients and due dates as a separate report, and a built in gestational wheel. But the ability to pull up the entire OB record in ACOG format is very impressive. Well done!

Pretty useless. This app was a good starting point but it appears that this is where they want to leave it. You can't view blocked-out time, can't access the patients' documents in their hub (other than encounters) and it's really basic. 

Improve it already!!

Needs work. I am a home care NP and I am frequently traveling and away from my computer. This app helps, but it needs A LOT of work. 

THE BAD: First of all, the search feature doesn't work. If you put in a name to search for, the app freezes and it has to be restarted. Thus, if the patient does not show up on my schedule, I cannot see their information. In addition, the schedule feature is unreliable.  On average only half of my patients scheduled for that day actually appear on my app schedule. For the patients that DO show up on my schedule, there are 3 icons that are displayed. First there is a camera icon, which is useless to me... What am I supposed to do? Ask my sick patient for a photo op in the middle of a visit? No, I don't think so. Second there is a 'person' icon, which I think is supposed to let you view the patient's information. However, it does not work. I click on it and again the app just freezes and has to be restarted. The third icon, the phone, I discuss in the section below. Moving on, the charge capture feature is useless to me and it doesn't work either. Again, if you try to search for a patient, it just freezes. I don't know about everybody else, but I don't see a need for this feature on a mobile app. I need this app to access important patient information and for essential communication with my co-workers and patients, NOT to bill visits. 

THE GOOD: The third icon on the patient screen in the schedule is a phone and it is the only useful one. With the phone icon,  I can call my patient with the push of a button. That is a very helpful feature to me, because I often have to call patients to let them know I'm on my way, or ask for directions to their home if I get lost. The app is good for messaging, reviewing labs, and for receiving telephone/web encounters. These features are definitely helpful when on the go, and they usually function properly.  

I cannot comment on the prescription refill feature, as my staff takes care of prescription refills for me.

ECW PLEASE FIX THIS APP!!!!!!

Meh. This app was all cool and everything when it first came out but they need to keep improving it if they want to keep up with the competition. Needs more functionality to truly function usefully after hours/on-call. Adapt or die eCW!

Please fix. It is impossible to add patients to your daily list through the app, it wants you to select "visit type" but nothing pops up. Also please add icd 10 codes. The app is so slow, takes almost 60 seconds to log in and I have a brand new iphone
Poor feedback. Very poor customer Svc, can not apply what it is supposed to do over all 3 out of 10 . Too many faults to mention. Care 360 much better
So-so. I appreciate the remote access, truly, but there are many frustrating limitations to what this app can do. And it wasn't easy to write this so-so review from the link provided

Glitches, poor service. I've been using ECW for three weeks and way unimpressed. It is full of excessive steps. The main reason we chose this was for the Scribe. This works poorly. It will not merge anything into the note except if dictated under HPI. Assessment and Treatment notes aren't saved anywhere. It glitches mid-dictation regularly and returns to home screen without saving anything.  We've sent numerous support orders but they are very unhelpful and even have told us that it is new to them as well and they aren't sure what do either. Don't go here. Stay away from ECW
Need URL Scheme Support. Please add the ability to launch the app via URL Scheme

What a tease. Rx sent as faxes don't arrive at pharmacy.  I pretty much use the mobile version as a "view only" app when on call and need to see the med hx.  It's ok to use to document telephone encounters.  iPad version is better but once again, you will be nickel and dimed

Typical for ecw. As with much of ecw, the promise is more than the reality: while the app promises to bring ecw to your phone, it is a clunky app that seldom works the way it's supposed to. The scribe functionality is not present despite the update! Another disappointment in a long line of disappointments from ecw
Great app. Review your schedule, send new scripts, get notifications of new labs available. Send refills

PATHETIC. what a sad and sorry shame! - this app could be REALLY useful and potentially a saving grace of the overall mothership program but instead [one assumes from lack resources put into its further development] it falls as far and flat as the mother. Ditto to comments below re need to input full details [vs PIN], excessive bugginess and, all in all, consummately dismal experience. Keep the day job, folks!
Cannot select a patient. Latest update does not allow for patient selection!

Very disappointing. The message screen doesn't work - I can see the messages on the web version but not on this app.  Charge entry is difficult and error prone. Rounding list is difficult to manage. ECW should do better!

How Do I Enter. This app deserves no star.  When I click "help" I get no help. The information is so small on the screen I need a magnifying glass to read it.  This app is not user friendly if I need to take an hour to learn how to register so I deleted the app.  This is a waste of time

Practice id. You ask for a Practice ID and the Help button try's to show a way to get one that only a Geek would understand!
Oh, and a computer is required to do this! On an iPhone app!
Idiot! Sick people are going to use this?
The only remedy is to have the programmer shot as a warning to others

Slow and unresponsive. I have used this both on a wifi network and LTE.  It seems to freeze continually and is slow other times.  I cannot navigate.  This needs a lot of work.  It looked promising, but if it takes so long, who cares
bad design, missing stuff, maybe next year (again). disappointed.  dinosaur only good on desktop. same old dated web stuff. where’s the original thinking

Nice UI, but missing one obvious item. I'm giving this app 4 stars, because, for what features it probably should have, it's nice. (In other words, I realize that an iPhone app for an EHR system is not supposed to "replace" the actual EHR, but simply provide some basic functions when you're on the go). 
That being said, it's a very nice improvement, especially in the UI, compared to using the eClinical Mobile "website bookmark" that merely opened safari on the iPhone. (It's an actual app). 
However, one of the main functions docs would use a mobile EHR app for is prescriptions. And someone in development FORGOT TO PUT THE OPTION FOR REFILLS in the eRx functionality (you can only send an eRx for something WITHOUT REFILLS?!)
Other than that obvious oversight, it's nice, and it shows that eCW is paying attention to the LOOK of the UI, something that is sorely lacking in the core PC program (which looks more like Minecraft than a 2014 EHR!)
Now your iPad has to be iOS 18. Everything was chill until you forced me to spend $500 on a new iPad that can update to IOS 18. I hate it when companies do this
MD. In ECT4, oftentimes the Loading time and not recognizing the user credentials are frustrating

eClinical app. Found the eClinical app to be short on detail. Specifically, it seems inordinately difficult to obtain patients records and to send in pharmaceutical items during the office hours.
Overall impression: not quite ready for primetime
Terrible. Because i enjoy fighting with a computer instead of treating patients
Horrible.. Avoid at all costs

Problems. This app needs to be redesigned with the physician workflow in mind. Physicians mainly want immediate access to the most recent note and the most recent studies performed. At present, there are too many options, too much nonessential content, and too many clicks to get to the information that actually matters.

On iPhone especially, the experience needs to be faster, cleaner, and more intuitive. A doctor should be able to open the app and, within 2 to 3 minutes, find everything clinically relevant without digging through excessive layers of navigation.

The platform should prioritize efficiency, simplicity, and rapid access to key patient information
Cecilia Grande MD. It’s extremely unreliable.  It’s constantly down
Log in. Not sure why we can’t have facial recognition to log into our app in 2026!
Glitches. When I am writing a note for recipient on an encounter with out ordering the medication first it’ll completely shut off the app
Sucks. Confusing, hard to navigate, too many ways to do one thing, not so very simple and fast for a fast paced office. Athena health way better. I promise

Fix a but, add a bug. For a little while it seemed that the app had improved but it has reverted to one glitch after another.  Each update fixes one problem but creates another.  The latest is not being able to view documents when default is latest and the app closing very frequently particularly when opening ICW that I can think of quickly.  It continues to not allow pasting because of “illegal characters”like those dastardly apostrophes.  I wish our company had gone with some other EMR but too late and too much sunk cost now

Last version was much better. This version requires more “clicks” than the last version.  The commonly used icons are hard to find. Making a telephone encounter is more cumbersome.  In clinic, I could use the last version without simultaneously running the desktop version.  Now, I have to run both in order to efficiently do both tasks.  The user should be able to run another program such as OpenEvidence without getting logged out of eclinical touch
Not intuitive. Cumbersome, repetitive, causing waste of time for the clinicians and doctors, tech support is as poor as could be

The worst. Continuous problems for months across multiple platforms including iPad, iPhone, now dealing with complete loss of dictation function, dropping valuable patient data.  On average I have to log out or restart devices 5-10 times a day to get the app to function.  Have replaced iPad, in-house IT have been troubleshooting our devices and Wi-Fi, and our side is fine.
Tell me it’s going to get better…

Can really help. It is a product that at times has been the best thing eCW does. It can really help with workflows and consents and is really nice for reducing the device between clinicians and patients. 
We all hate to document, but this is a tool that makes it better

Buggy. Clean interface, but in a fast paced clinical environment, I cannot be waiting around for the next window to load. Often times, it’s faster to force quit the app and restart than to wait for the app to load a window. I also love the ability to use dot phrases on the desktop cloud version, a feature that is sorely missed in ECT4. I tried to give this an honest effort as a replacement for the desktop version on my laptop, however, it’s not efficient enough to be worth the switch

Return back to the functionality of version 3.9. EClinicalTouch Version 3.9 is Way better than 4.0.  The same functionality 3.9 had needs to be returned to ECT 4.0. As it is right now I have to go back to a desktop or a laptop to be as efficient as I was with ECT version 3.9.  I’ve spoken with the ECT development team and they say by ECT version 4.17, most of the 3.9 functionalities will return (like being able to do everything on one screen, instead of having to go back-and-forth between screens).  Until then, sadly, I have to rely on a desktop to be efficient with the amount of workload I have.  (I’ll update this review once 4.17 comes out.)

Not good. Try to avoid this system at all costs. This has made scanning papers turn into a whole process. Too many buttons to click and the accessibility is not there either. The whole system is slow and I have run into many problems and system errors with it since transitioning to it in July of this year. Poorly made. Poorly managed

App design. Overall it’s a much better app than v1.  However it still needs to be less clicks.  Think about the most common reasons a physicians needs the app. When on call.  And the most common thing we should be able to one click is send a Rx or Lab to any place anytime or to see a document and medical hx summary.   It takes way too many clicks to do that now.  Plus there are limits who you can send to.  There should be a blank fax number field where we can put any number, doesn’t have to be someone prepopulated in our local version.  Being to Call patient directly from within the app by clicking their number BUT too patient call originates from office or a masked number would be helpful since we don’t call patients from our personal phones even though that is where the app resides. A nice feature would be able to send a quick typed up letter with doctors signature to a patient would be a nice feature (sick note, jury duty letter) and inter office texting.  BTW, the admin should be able to see all provider schedules from the app.   Overall great job.  Definite better than 2 stars, at least a 3.  Make these click efficiencies and you are talking 5 star app

Update to v4.24.2 is a train wreck. Version 4.14.2 is a train wreck. HPI used to be editable by line in Preview tab. Now it is not editable at all. It has to be edited on the horrendous Progress Note tab, on which you cannot edit by line

Truly unhelpful app. I don’t know what’s worse, the fact that this app is beyond unhelpful in a clinical setting or that you have to pay a monthly charge to use it. Lol! It has limited functionality, its not user-friendly, and it logs off the second you have to tab away and use another function on your device. The constant need to log in rendered this app practically useless for me. I mostly use it to document images of skin and msk conditions and to check my schedule for the next day. Medical field is too fast paced for this clunky inefficient app. There are literally free hipaa compliant apps that are more helpful. Quite unfortunate

Improved. I’ve been using clinical touch for a while now, and the recent updates have made a noticeable difference. The app feels more stable and has become easier to navigate. I especially like the new customization options for layout. I also recently found out that prisma is now available, which has been a useful addition. It’s good to see the app continuing to improve in meaningful way

Terrible. Just awful since the update. It takes forever to load and do anything. For reasons that are not clear, prescriptions will just not randomly be able to be sent to the pharmacy. The ridiculous requirements on prescriptions such as you can’t use the word in or as are so frustrating. I really only use this when I’m on vacation. I cannot expect any person to ever use the iPad app to document real Notes. Probably will have to bring laptop on Vacation from now on. Stay away stay far away.

It may be fixable but I really don’t wanna spend 30 minutes with someone I can barely understand on the phone.  Half the time they can’t fix the problem

iPad and tech support terrible. I beg any physician or administrator thinking about this EMR to run away from this product as fast as you can. Tech support terrible. iPad extremely glitchy and slow. If you are lucky to get someone from tech support you can even understand, good luck getting them to even understand the problem you are trying to tell them about much les have the issue resolve. Please don’t use this EMR. You can spend half as much on an equally horrible product but at least you saved some money
Barely useful. Hardly useful due to frequent crashes, and slowness

What a disaster. I thought this app would be something outstanding, but what a disaster! It is slow and unresponsive. Besides being unresponsive, eCW blocked viewing anything if the provider is not enrolled/subscribed to eClinicalTouch. 

I would love to give Zero Star! Get your act together eCW!
I like Sunoh.ai integration. Works very well. Nice integration

I like it. I think this version works better than the previous version. There is a learning curve involved (just like previous versions), but overall I think the UI is much more intuitive.  Couple of wishlist things: 1. There should be a “default” button for each individual item in the Examination section rather than only being able to “merge” all available defaults. 2. When view past encounters for a patient, you should be able to swipe right or left to scroll between the different encounters; currently, you can view the past encounter, then you have to close it and reopen the list to choose another one
User unfriendly. Difficult to navigate, is not intuitive.  Would expect this to mirror the desktop,  but doesn’t in the least.  Please allow eClinical in the Google Chrome APP

Switching from 3.9 to 4. This update is the worst.It is slow,cumbersome,and almost impossible to get anything done.The quick order magnifying glass does not contain the orders that I need and I don’t understand the quick note functionality.My app doesn’t even fit my iPad screen as the shots on this preview show. I had a training session webinar and the trainer ,although very helpful on some issues,told me to reply to a patient web message by writing in the blue block and hitting done,which is totally incorrect.You need to use the speech balloon to reply…which comes up only after hitting the w.Please eClinicalTouch this is a nightmare…..and a huge medicolegal risk when your trainers are giving practitioners wrong information

Notifications & App slowness. First, I use the desktop, iPad and phone as part of my workflow.  As most of us have done, I have created a workflow that will allow me function efficiently in the office setting (using the desktop and iPad).  The new version 4.0 has been an upgrade in some ways in regards to iOS, but a downgrade in others.  
For example, why do we no longer receive alert/notifications via the iPhone app? It was GREAT to know when a message/encounter/lab came in with the previous app, so that I could reply to it in an expedited manner. However, now there are now alerts and I have log in every so often to see if anything new is there.  
While the iPhone/iPad app has increased functionality (which I love particularly with the phone), in regards to making it more practical to use when out of the office, the app is also slow compared to previous versions!  Please fix this! 
Also, there should be an option on how documents to list then in order of last received, instead of only by folder.  Having to find “new” documents by scrolling the others that have been reviewed is time consuming in the office setting.  Please make this an option

Would give ZERO stars if that was an option. This is a horrendous update that absolutely destroys the ability to write clinical notes efficiently. It’s unbelievable that the company did not run a beta test which would have elucidated the countless problems that clinicians and end users now face. eCW has shown a disdain for it’s customers by releasing this version. 
They need to restore the old version and flush this version down the toilet. 
Extremely disappointed and frustrated
Bad update. Bad update, everything was fine. This is now causing new problems. Wrong patients merging, appointments not showing up, exct

Worst app I’ve ever used. By far the worst app ever. Constantly crashing, and when it works it’s extremely slow. Please fix this. Currently it’s doing its favorite pastime with the wheel of death, not logging in. Typical

Who decided this was good?. I assume with the resources eClinical has they would have taken the opinion of people using the software to improve it.  EVERYTHING IS HARDER TO DO WITH THIS VERSION.  Also all the 2 star reviews are frankly being too generous, when you read their reviews you see them using the desktop/web version more.  The whole point of the touch version is to get things done not to go back to the desktop.  Refills are a nightmare.  Reviewing telephone encounters is cumbersome, writing prescriptions and other orders are also unnecessarily complicated

ECW 4 for iPad. This company seems to try to continually worsen its products.
From the non intuitive nature, its upgrades vastly altering its internal logic and the inconsistencies from one product line to another, it’s clear to me that they don’t care about the end user

Downgrade from 3.9. Hi, I have been using the app with templates and the Nuance cloud dictation for years in a very busy practice. The current iteration of 4.0 limits the customizable nature of the scribe function (listing which subheading to schribe under. The app has always been Half-baked and glitchy compared to the PC enabled version, but the scribe function umps to the top of the dictation every time I put in a new heading .  Hopefully the Ai Bot version is better, although not enabled for us yet
Much better than v3.. Much quicker and even though I havent been trained on it, its so far relatively intuitive. But the speed for me is much much improved

They broke it. The upgrade looks nicer, but it is much slower. I used EClinicalTouch extensively prior to upgrading to ECW V12, and now I have had to abandon it. The order entry is painfully slow, constantly locks up the treatments with concurrency, even when you have been out of the chart for several hours. Hopefully the fix it in the future, I would love to use it again. Until then, I have completely abandoned and gone back to taking a laptop into the room with me

3.9 was much better. eClinicalTouch3 was responsive and great!  Version 4 so unstable, also do not understand why iPad eClinicalTouch cannot be built upon a solid foundation and have enhancements added.  Each version is literally a new app that is built from scratch.  Essentially starting over each and every time - seems inefficient.  Epic Mychart, ecw healow, ecw kiosk, Microsoft Office and many other productivity apps appear to start with solid app and then build upon it - not having users uninstalling and redeploying a new app.  

Also eClinicalTouch is sold as full charting on a iPad during office visits, it is not unreasonable to expect this

TERRIBLE. They took a wonderful app and absolutely ruined it. It is much slower to use. Unfortunately, it is closer to the desktop version of the software which is not a good thing. Please let me go back to the old version. This is intolerable

Very Slow. We upgraded to the newest version three weeks ago. Having been very efficient with the previous version and enjoying it more than the desktop version, I have to say that the opposite is true here. Running on my iPad Pro, this version runs much slower, with longer loading times in between screens (it times out with an error 20% of the time. It also has taken many normal practice functions, and made them require far more on the screen. One example: trying to fill a prescription request from my inbox rather than it bringing up the patient’s med list right on that screen, I’m forced to go to the virtual visit and pull the meds in there. The notes also take a good amount longer, as the individual load screens just take longer to get done. Another is that while you’re dictating, if a character such as a degree sign is placed, it automatically deletes your whole paragraph rather than notifying you of this to correct- highly annoying. The billing format is also worse, as it is harder to enter all of the diagnosis codes in than it was previously. Hopefully these problems will be addressed in future updates, but right now, I find myself having to go back to the desktop version more to get my work done. As others have said, it should be made easier, not harder to get things done

Too many taps, runs slower. The previous version (before 4) was so much better.  Very efficient workflows, far faster, far fewer clicks to do almost anything.

Version 4 is slower, less intuitive, decreases productivity, increases frustration

Not sure what all this people are talking about.. Firstly, “Yes, eClinicalTouch 3.9 is faster. Hopefully, they can improve this.”

“My friends and colleagues, you have a fully functional EMR on a smartphone. You can do practically everything in eClinicalTouch 4 on a smartphone. A smartphone is not the best way to write progress notes for 40 patients, but I am sure that if you pass the learning curve, it is very doable. eClinicalMobile is a joke compared to the functionality that eClinicalTouch 4 gives you on a smartphone. I feel there is a good balance for this version to work seamlessly on both devices, i.e., iPad and iPhone. I will look forward to some improvements in the speed of data processing on the iPad.”

“Now, why do you want to write notes using eClinicalTouch 4 on an iPad when the web-based version can give you all and more? Again, if you pass the learning curve, you can document on the iPad as fast as the web-based full version. I feel this is a great improvement overall. I can e-message my patients, renew prescriptions, review documents, fax progress notes to other providers, complete notes, or billing, and much more from my iPhone.”

“Please, please, someone explain to me the previous reviews. Respectfully yours.”

OMG, utter failure. What have you done!! Version 3.9 was workable, just needed a few tweaks but this Version 4 is completely useless with all beginner’s mistakes. What were you thinking, who made this?? How could you have carelessly released such a nonfunctional inefficient piece of junk. You expect people to pay for this?

Go back to latest version. The pros to this update are minuscule compared to the MULTITUDE of cons that the update brought. As mentioned in other reviews, the iOS version (iPad/iPhone) is trash. It takes 3-4x more clicks now to do anything. And as you’re completing your note, after each section is complete, it takes you back to the top of the note….the whole thing is inefficient
Garbage. less the said the better i think

Not loving this version. I used to be quite efficient in touch.  While some enhancements are helpful (reducing concurrency locks in TEs across platforms) and ability to view the resource schedule, I'm finding this MUCH less efficient. I'll admit there's always a learning curve, but placing orders as a physician is unnecessarily difficult.    Bring back some of that ease of use, and I'll up the stars. 

Also, sadly more clicks/taps...  aren't we supposed to be working to reduce these?

Terrible upgrade. Have been using EClinical touch for almost 8 years. This is by far the worst version yet. All the flexibility of using IOS have been taken away with this version. It has been come as cumbersome as using the windows version. This is basically a simple version of the windows platform with all the extra clicking and none of the benefits of using an iPad. I have stopped using this and have gone back to the laptop version. I watched all the videos and tried to make it as flexible as possible but have realized that my notes take longer and are not as complete. Definitely a major disappointment would definite not recomend this upgrade
Terrible APP. They turned a good ECW  app into a terrible App they should be fired and sued for the crap application they forced providers into. If you are not already a ECW company DO NOT BUY!!!!!

Horrible. This app is so bad it’s hard to believe it was developed by professionals and is used by so many doctors. What a freaking mess. Where this really got obvious was when a new doctor used a different app that isn’t a navigational mess

The support service was responsive but unhelpful, suggesting I contact my providers and reenter everything. I’m not sure why they abdicated their role to patients and providers to fix the problem. Can no longer use app due to Terms - Terms Breech HIPAA. I have let my Provider and Practice know that I can no longer use the app Healow because i cannot accept the Terms & Conditions. READ THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS PEOPLE. While I am not directly negatively affected by the Terms other Patients are & they are negatively affected. The current section about Teens and accessing their Patient Portals MUST be re-written before I can accept any Terms. Teens DO NOT need permission from their Parents to create and access their Patient Portal. Teens do need to take the time and think before granting either parent access to the Patient Portal. Attn. Teenagers: You DO NOT have to give your Parents access to your Patient Portal. If you are a Teenager and you are forced to give up your medical record to your parents due to this app then you can take people to court for a HIPAA Privacy breech and forced coercion. Any Parent who takes this access without informing the Teen they do not have to give Parents access is breaking the law

Same bad quirks and bugs after 2 years. This app, the browser-based EMR, and Practice Management program all seem stuck in time both design-wise and with updates. For example I’ve been waiting for months for a fix to this app’s “internal server error issue” that prevents me from processing or even seeing refill requests. No dice. Entering an rx and want to edit the number of refills? It’s not a drop down, it’s this awkward open-text field in which I can never erase the zero so I can either prescribe zero refills or 10 refills. Need to know the time at which you saw your patient for the note you signed a few weeks ago? In both the app and the browser it doesn’t show anywhere — you have to back all the way out and go to your schedule, scroll back and find that date of service, because if you go to the patient demographics, then appt hx, sometimes it won’t display enough history to find that encounter. Want to finalize a superbill from the app? Bummer, I get the same internal server error as with refills. I’ve sent lists of things like this to Kareo and never heard back including when I’ve offered to spend the time to go through it all in detail. So, on Jan 1 2022 I’m starting a different EMR. Don’t waste your time here, trust me

Terrible app needs lots of work. Crashes, freezes, and in general is difficult to get anything done in a timely fashion. Just about done with it, not sure how much more I can take. Visually intuitive which is the only reason it gets 2 stars from me

Great when it works. It often has chart load errors not allowing me to complete my notes on my device. When it’s working it’s easy to use and has everything I need, but I’ve considered changing EHRs altogether just to have a more reliable app!

Kareo can’t handle their growth. Kareo has many problems. But worse, they are too overwhelmed to handle them or answer our requests for help.

Use this app single day all the time. I use this Kareo App every single day of the week. From refilling prescriptions, checking on the financial status of my practice, to scheduling, charting, uploading documents, viewing patients medical record, reviewing last office note, charting vital signs to chart/graph improvements or worsening blood pressure and weights. Communication with patients and staff via the messaging. I’ve got all three modules at no cost as long as I hired them to do my billing which they do a great job. They are continuing to improve the app on a regular basis, are working on bugs to fix them. I use the iPad as well as iPhone versions. All my staff uses the apps and prefer it to the desktop or web app version. I upload my diagnostic images from my X-ray machine right into their charts. I use the iPhone voice recognition to dictate my notes. Can’t say enough good about this app. Have used it since Nov 2017 and man has it gotten better with the improvements. 5 stars isn’t enough

Update: still a terrible company. At one time this company offered free use of their software and promised me it would always be free. They explained that they make their money through the billing upgrades or the marketing upgrade that they offered for a reasonable price. They offered me a free trial of their marketing program which was nice but because I had just started out I canceled before the trial ended. A few months later they sent an email saying they were going to start charging for the practice management ehr module that was previously free. I hadn’t been using the software and at the time I didn’t read the email. Later they charged me $199 on the card I gave for the marketing trial. I never authorized any purchase and was outraged. I talked to them in the phone and they fixed things and said it would continue to be free. I still was not using the software but a few months later they charged me again. I had talked to them on the phone and emailed some (unnecessary) angry things telling them they needed to refund my money that they were not authorized to charge. They did not refund it even though they never should have had the card number in the first place

Kareo, this is ridiculous. Please fix it

Great so far, with two suggestions. This far this has been a great app to view our patient schedules and associated documentation. I have two suggestions for improvements.

1) A 4-digit pin is inadequate considering the sensitive information contained with a practice's account. I suggest a 6 or 8-digit pin, or the option of a swipe pattern, for both fast access and security.

2) When viewing the calendar when I click on an item it displayed the patient's general information. At the bottom left is the "go to chart" link. Clicking that takes me to the patient's chart. However there's no "back" button to return me to where I was. So I have to navigate manually back to the calendar. This is a real pain especially when I'm looking at calendar appointments weeks into the future because i must scroll to find where I was before

Do not choose EHR or App!. Won't even finish downloading and I've tried for 3 days! Does not surprise me as I've used their EHR software for 18 months with nothing but headaches. Only wanted the app to close out my charts faster and not deal with software issues. If you're shopping for an EHR do NOT waste your time and money!
Great mobile offering. I love the app, it has gotten smoother and I can get everything done from here

Terrible user interface and service.. I have been a Kareo subscriber for over 4 years now. The App itself has been absolutely terrible in terms of true functionality and design. Touch ID hardly works correctly (all other of my apps work great) and after I enter in my routine password every time --it then crashes and have to log in again but then crashes again. This happens several times ...I then just give me and then just spend extra time to call my office to ask for an updated daily schedule. When I need to look up a patient's number to call them, it crashes again after trying to access the demographic sections. Then I waste more time and have to call my office to get a patient's phone number. This has happened version after version for several years of Kareo EHR app "updates"--yes I I have an updated iPhone 6s and 10.3.3.   Kareo can't even get these basics down on an app--who cares about bells and whistles features. As a real busy doctor,I can't afford the time to waste my efforts and workflow time. Can you?
