Originally started with their billing services platform and found they were completely irresponsible and billed the wrong diagnostic codes ( despite giving them a clear table of my top 50 ICD9/10 codes) and couldn't understand billing properly blue shield and Medicaid patients. After repeated phone calls and emails to Vice Presidents of "customer success" and operations , Kareo still was not adept on submitting properly billing. I lost dozens of hours of time and tens of thousands of revenue income as a result. Most of their claims input is done in India and their staff was not trained on proper submission of claims. They were like robots and did adapt to my individual practice despite "individual billing services" platform. In addition they also got rid of phone support and went to a ticket system--which takes at least 4-5 days to get a response on a billing issue---then have to wait another 4-5 days after I respond. 

I then downgraded to just Practice Management with Kareo ...but since the App is a total disaster, I am completely dissolving my account. Because of these primary reasons--can't access my patient demographics or my schedule on the fly, I am cancelling my Kareo account completely. We have switched to a much more user intuitive platform --drchrono. Log in and demographic information is very accessible and has a beautiful user interface. 

Look at the other Kareo App reviews and you will find opinions that voice similar concerns about Kareo App and Service as well. (:

iPhone app is of limited use. The web based EHR offers all the necessities at no cost which is great. I implemented the EHR in my practice due to the advertised native iPhone and iPad apps. When I installed the iPhone app I was dismayed to see that it is little more than a schedule checker and old note viewer. Without the ability to start and sign new SOAP notes, this app is essentially worthless on the phone. Supposedly the iPad app is fully functional but I really think that the company shouldn't omit the limitations of the iPhone app in their advertising. I'm not sure if there are technical limitations to having a fully functional native iPhone interface but there is no excuse for actively promoting it as such. The website makes it look like you can access the notes features by phone and conveniently omits that it is read only.  If you are shopping for an EHR that works seamlessly with iOS, just be sure you intend to purchase a newer iPad with Touch ID. Any mobile device other than that will simply leave you disappointed

Kareo is keeping with the times!. I am very impressed at the improvements the team have been making in having patient care more fluent and efficient. I can now take all of the vitals on my smartphone and that plays a huge part in my role as CA, so I appreciate that I can now file everything I need to, on the go. Just the other day, I had one patient walk in during two new patients and because I was capable of working off of more than one device, I was able to efficiently and effectively add all the vitals and the walk-in on the fly! I also love the idea that you branched out towards Apple Watch and applaud the attempt at the ideal of being notified five minutes before any scheduled appointment. I can not wait until you work out all the kinks to it so I can take advantage of it. I also greatly appreciate two new features on the dashboard area on the Kareo Practice site:

Add new appointment is amazing for adding appointments in a swift manner. Instead of clicking on the dashboard and clicking on calendar, the immediate flip to the calendar site is really helpful and not to mention its ability to flip back to dashboard when the appointment has been added. 

Next nice add on has got to be the “Schedule Follow-Up Appointment” feature that is available after Checking Out the patient. The fact that it remembers the patient, along with the practice and the physician they work with shaves precious time when I’m putting them in. 

I also like how when you set up the next appointment, it will flag you if a patient owes money and provides you (if you click the checkmark) a way to immediately go into the submitting pay area. Only thing I’d like to see in that respect is for this option to show up regardless whether a patient owes or not, as sometimes the treatments may not have been put in yet to accurately display what is truly owed. 

All in all Kareo has done well to evolve and adapt with this generation. I look forward to benefiting from their future endeavors

Cannot recommend. To purchasing several iPads for one of our test clients we found this application lacks several key features and in general is buggy.

A for instance would be the lack of ability to check patients in for treatment on the app if the appointment was scheduled on the desktop application or the website as a recurring visit.  

When we asked Kareo support to correct this flaw Bridgette's response was that it would be too much work.  That response surprised us with the amount of promotion they devote to the iPad only application.

The bugs in this app and the lackluster customer support make it impossible to recommend this app and therefore this practice management software.  If they fix it in an update I will change my review

Ups & Downs. I’ve been using drchrono since 2013. Frequent bugs in new releases to data being entered but just never saved, or scripts being sent that never arrived. I was seriously looking at EPIC as an alternative. RECENTLY drchrono seems to be investing a lot of time in updating its whole platform and making it internally more cohesive and an overall smoother user experience. drchrono seems to have gotten its second wind. I’m hopeful, but our transition to EPIC is still slated for Feb 2027 with one eye still on drchrono. App doesn’t support external keyboards.. I submitted a support ticket about physical keyboards not properly working on the iPad EHR app. I use an iPad Magic Keyboard pro for iPad as well as Bluetooth keyboards. Both don’t work properly when typing into their macro search feature on iPad. 

Support response is that drchrono doesn’t support external keyboards. Yikes. 

Lack of keyboard support is odd for a modern EHR and everyone should know this before integrating drchrono this into their clinics. Hopefully one day they will add support in the future or at least notify users that their app doesn’t work properly with keyboards. 

For the record, all keyboards previously functioned properly in previous versions but it seems they will be dropping it in the current and future releases. 

Here is their response:

“We’re following up regarding the issue you reported where macros become hidden when using a Bluetooth [or external] keyboard. After reviewing this with our development and product team, we can confirm that Bluetooth [and external] keyboards are not currently supported in the DrChrono application, and there are no plans at this time to add support for this functionality.

As a result, to ensure proper macro behavior and a consistent experience, we recommend using the application without a Bluetooth [or physical] keyboard.

Thank you for your understanding, as well as your time and patience.”

I hope they change their mind, but until they do, every current and prospective user should know about this limitation

Very low rated EHR. Terrible tech support. At times it takes a long time to correct mishaps by low quality platform. Used to be an excellent EMR but unfortunately it wend down after new company took over it

Clinical interface now failing.. I have been with Dr Chrono for over seven years. When I first started working with them they were a startup and they had a lot of energy and they were willing to work hard for my business. We had a good relationship for a lot of years whenever there was a problem with the interface they were quick to fix and upgrade the system. The last two years have been quite a bit of frustration. Both on the clinical interface and the backend business interface, we have had tickets go neglected for a month at a time. Support is nonexistent. We have called numerous times only to be told they are “working on it.” as an alternative to EPIC we initially felt that the hiccups associated with Dr. Chrono were tolerable. Now that EPIC has made significant strides in the outpatient arena, these problems can no longer be overlooked. The value proposition of integration associated with an EPIC transition is now making more sense.  I am sorry to be another practice that has to integrate into the monolith that is EPIC but these little guys have dropped the ball

Several Issues. The 16 length password is too much. The system doesn’t leave you signed in for 30 days like it advises. . Labs don’t cross over. Patients have problems signing up for the portal. When scheduling a break, it only has minutes as an option, not hours

Unauthorized charges. We had been a long term customer but they started charging automated unauthorized charges on our credit card and it has been still going. They also raised the monthly fees more than 50% without your agreement and charge duplicated reminder text that the system created. The worst part is they do not respond to the inquiries on those major issues many months.  Also they do not give you back our/patients data under their name and will ask you to pay. ——-If you feel you need additional support from our Professional Services team in navigating your files, you have the option of purchasing an hour to meet with our team at a rate of $500/hour.—-

If you do not want to get overcharged, good faith and honest customer care and protect your and patients data, then please choose other EHR system

Avoid at all costs!. The DrChrono mobile app is absolutely one of the worst medical apps I've used. The  app support team is terrible —they take forever to respond, and when they do, their solutions are unhelpful and show zero understanding of urgent medical workflow needs.

The mobile interface is painful to use - basic tasks like checking schedules or updating patient notes require way too many taps and often freeze mid-action. The app regularly crashes, especially during patient encounters, and the layout feels like it's stuck in 2010. Simple features like uploading photos or accessing documents are unnecessarily complicated.

Syncing is a major issue- changes made on the mobile app often don't show up on the desktop version for hours, which is unacceptable in a medical setting. Push notifications are unreliable, and the app frequently logs you out at the most inconvenient times.

For medical professionals who need a reliable mobile solution for their practice, avoid the DrChrono app completely

Despite marketing itself as a modern healthcare solution, this app falls drastically short. The premium price tag is absolutely not justified by this subpar mobile experience. I strongly recommend looking elsewhere for your practice's mobile EHR needs

Physiatrist who converted from Aprima EHR. We have been with DrChrono for six months, and I have to totally sing the praises of this cloud-based program. I am a longtime PC user but most of the time I’m simply on my iPhone getting my work done.

It’s an extremely intuitive interface and once you set up the templates for your clinical work, things can really sing along.

Some things I would love to have, but it might be my ignorance at this time that I don’t have them:

#1 Straightforward way of tracking procedures we have performed by date and by procedure type.
This type of mini-database would be helpful as we do various procedures in our clinic focused on physiatry.

#2 More powerful editing tool for putting in photographic or imaging data. This is light years ahead of what we had with Aprima, which was a disaster, but I am getting greedy and now I want more features!

#3 The medical voice recognition program built into the app is a bit primitive. One is not able to save vocabulary, train, etc. It does repeatedly annoying things, but I can get over this. I use transcription for the heavy lifting in any event, such as new patients.

—————
All in all, it’s been a very good transition. I barely have looked at a user manual or online video.

One more thing, I can count on two hands a number of times I’ve ever put in an online review for anything, so this means something for me. I want to share my good experience.

Richard in Seattle

amazing EMR. I am a practicing ER doctor who owns his own urgent care center and I have been through several EMRs before finding Dr. Chrono. Dishonest billing, almost non-existent support. RUN! Please read!. I was a VERY early adopter of this EHR system, and in the beginning they were lovely to work with. EHRs can be very difficult to adapt to and adopt- so the personal one-on-one assistance was terrific. 
I believe they are under different ownership/management at this point, and they’ve become an absolute nightmare. Word to the wise: DO NOT SUBSCRIBE- you will regret it.
They employ dishonest billing practices- forcing you to pay unexplained invoices in order to unlock the system. We were provided with a $1600 invoice at the beginning of May- they told us it was a past due balance. They send a warning when you are 30 days past due-so there is literally no way to accumulate a balance that high. We paid the balance because we had to in order to have access to our system. I was told this brought our balance to $0. Lo and behold- I get an additional invoice for over $600 during the SAME MONTH. I then also find out that we are being billed for a second user which we cancelled months ago, who has NEVER logged in NOT ONCE, and they even better- they won’t delete him until OCTOBER.
This was once a terrific service- now it’s just an awful joke. STAY AWAY AT ALL COSTS!

Disaster $80000 legal bill. This is a review of my experience using this app for more than 5 years.

Last year I encountered locked clinical notes, created using this app, for the same visit with the same digital signature and time stamp that had different clinical data.  Yes.  A supposedly unalterable document had been altered. By software. Continuously LOCKED and SIGNED clinical notes made using this app for a visit had the same digital, time-stamped signature with DIFFERENT clinical data at different times.

This problem would be similar to using the popular app to sign real estate deals and then your realtor telling you that the app changed the contract after you signed it with no audit trail.

They say they are not responsible for your data while using this app (Service agreement (ii) and (iii). We thought it prudent to backup our data.
They offer box.com to backup clinical notes and documents. (See drchrono Customer Success > Getting Started > Getting Setup > How do I backup all of my information within drchrono

Yet, when box doesn’t work their attorney says “[he] claims that drchrono ‘is required to fix this problem.’  “This is not true.  Under the Terms of Service, which are prominently disclosed on drchrono’s website, drchrono and its affiliates:

MAKE NO WARRANTY THAT (i) THE DRCHRONO SERVICE WILL MEET YOUR REQUIREMENTS, (ii) THE DRCHRONO SERVICE WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED, TIMELY, SECURE, OR ERROR-FREE, (iii) THE RESULTS THAT MAY BE OBTAINED FROM THE USE OF THE DRCHRONO SERVICE WILL BE ACCURATE OR RELIABLE, (iv) THE QUALITY OF ANY GOODS OR SERVICE AVAILABLE ON THE DRCHRONO SERVICE WILL MEET YOUR EXPECTATIONS, (v) THE DRCHRONO CURRENCY HAS ANY VALUE, AND (vi) ANY ERRORS IN THE SERVICE WILL BE CORRECTED.”

You don’t realize what is at risk until something goes wrong.  Drchrono’s intransigence toward data corruption and their responsibility to fix it is astonishing. Why would anyone use this product?

Because of data errors while using this app, our legal bills for representation against drchrono are close to $80000 so far. We are a two provider practice.

While using this app and trying to correct the note corruption issue, we inexplicably experienced all our support tickets disappearing and our box backup stopped working.

This is no joking matter.  Using this app practically destroyed our practice.  Would you ever want to do business with a company that won’t stand behind their own errors?  Particularly when it comes to medical records?

Poor customer support. I have never left a 1 Star review before.

There is no number to call when you need support, they lock you into contracts that automatically renew for a full year (month to month is not an option, even if you have the employee just for another month) and putting contracts on hold until you hire another doctor is not an option. Have been having an issue that’s been unresolved and apparently they’re understaffed. Won’t be renewing when this contract is up despite of the difficulty of switching EHR systems

Read Before You Buy. I have used this app for over 5 years to complete all my clinical notes at our practice.  Last year we encountered an issue where – over time – a clinical note with the same digital signature and same time stamp had different clinical data. These notes were shared outside our office to other professionals and to the patients before we caught the data issue.

The best analogy I can come up with is to imagine you are buying a house and you sign your contract online.  Subsequently, the realtor calls and says the computer program generated two different contracts with your exact same digital signature.  Now apply this scenario to medical data.

Drchrono’s response was nothing less than abhorrent. They admitted on our first call with their first of at least three attorneys that other practices were impacted.  They repeatedly started most conversations with some form of statement that they were not responsible to fix data problems per the service agreement.

After one-year and almost $80,000 in legal bills we got our notes corrected as best our legal team thought possible per CMS guidelines.  We added an amendment to each corrupt note and notified all patients. In retaliation for not signing an agreement to not report the problem to the OCR, drchrono gave us 30-day’s notice and shut off our service. 

All I can recommend is to read the service agreement.  It is egregious and they really mean it when they say there is no warranty that “ANY ERRORS IN THE SERVICE WILL BE CORRECTED.” Second, make sure you have a data integrity rider in your malpractice policy.  

Choosing an EMR is one of the most difficult decisions to make for a medical practice.  Using this app literally cost tens of thousands of dollars in legal bills and hundreds (and I mean hundreds) of additional hours beyond the typical 12-hour days.

If you happen to be reading this and are a current drchrono user, I would suggest you contact drchrono and ask if your notes were impacted or altered in any way without your knowledge

Long time user.. I have used this successfully since October 2012. The company has gone through a lot of growth since then and there were a lot of growing pains when I first started. I will say they always did scramble to get things right and they have kept their word on several things that date back to problems that occurred during these early years. For that I am grateful.

SALES FORCE: I have found them honest and straightforward. The really isn’t much to it. The plants are fairly straightforward and simple but you really should carefully scrutinize the plans before you choose an option. However it is simple to switch. They were open to negotiations regarding changing a few features or allowing me extra volumes on some features since I was not using others. Also they have kept their word on pricing. On rare occasions I have another doctor cover for me and I did not want to pay the entire fee for an additional person. They accommodated us. They gave me a reasonable discount.

SUPPORT:  I customize all my templates. It’s the nature of what I do. So this may or may not apply to you. And this is a terrific feature. However it is this area that was the weakest but has slowly ground with fits and starts in tween much improved system. It is nearly impossible to call anybody at support. They call you after an email. They do it on their own time. That time is almost never good since we obviously are in practices that are busy. You would think they would understand that allowing us to call them and reach a person would be imperative.  When a connection is finally made the solution is either a hand and the support person knows it or it is turned over to developers. That’s where the trouble can really start! However I find this a universal problem regarding clinical practice of medicine and development of Internet technologies.

Going back to the telephone issue I have found it very frustrating to be forced to write the issue in an email type response system only to have a back-and-forth where the support person completely misses the point time and time again. Then they might ask you for screenshots. Then for sessions just sit down and they can look at your desktop remotely. But to do that you have to get an appointment that you scheduled online through their web based system. Quite often their appointment times were not at all convenient and overlapped mostly into clinical hours. It could take a long long time to get a point across. I do think the people that work there are quite good and just caught up in the system that is dysfunctional. However it seems I have been assigned a particular person finally to oversee the account and they have merged past issues so that person can handle them. I was promised this in the past and it never materialized. It seems to be well on its way at the moment. I am very pleased with this change.

DEVELOPERS: it seems they have different teams for different parts of their product. They have a phone app that is extremely good but does not work exactly like the iPad app and there are limitations of the phone app that do not live up to the iPad app and vice versa! To make matters even more confusing if you use the web based browser on your desktop there are even different features there. I really think they need to make this process a lot better so that you can do anything on the desktop that you can do on the iPad and vice versa. And I mean ANYTHING. It is extremely difficult to enter ICD 10 codes and CPT codes and HCPCS codes on the desktop and phone. It’s a cinch on the iPad though you have to use the desktop to set it up initially if you want to create picklist of options to make it simple. They are sort of like playlists on iTunes. 

The browser that works best on the desktop seems to change from year to year and I have had breakdowns of the system when I use Chrome lately and breakdowns in the past using Safari. This might be an indication that the company is still young and going through transitions and not able to.all their eyes and cross all their cheese. I do not consider this an issue such that I would not use the system.  But it is very frustrating.

GENERAL COMMENTS:
This does not have a robust feature for tracking each change made in the medical record. In particular the templates are use in essence show no detailed tracking at all. You can get an audit of the activity but it is incomplete so it will show major things such as who opened for lock to note. I think this feature really needs to be fixed. I know there is a lawsuit in New York state that involved in medical record company not accurately tracking things and it turned out to be a huge fine or penalty in the hundreds of millions of dollars. Dr. Chrono can probably do a better job than that company. I think they already are. But this needs to be improved for us to feel safe about who is gaining access to the EHR and what changes are specifically being made. The tracking is much better if you do not use custom templates. But since custom templates are featured, it should have this ability throughout the system.

If two users on different iPads are on the same page it reeks havoc on the data. You can lose an entire page. Data loss can also occur on the desktop. You have to be very careful to make sure when you are on the desktop that what you entered was actually excepted. There is a small bright red notification that will appear if the data was not excepted but it is hidden if you are scrolled and working at the bottom of the note. This is a rather astonishingly difficult thing to work with. I have lost long dictation/typed in text entries because of this. The desktop based work I perform is when I need to do a lot of free hand typing. It’s pretty common to have to do so and if I can’t rely on the EHR successfully storing this information then it becomes extremely frustrating. 

I have more to say, but there seems to be a limit on what I can enter

User Friendly. I’ve used other EHR’ and EMR’s program and this is the best one I’ve used so far. It’s very user friendly. It’s easy to navigate. Best part of it you can access it anywhere. All the patient information you need is all in one application. No need to file and pulling up patient’s chart. I highly recommend this

No clinical awareness. There is clearly no one on their staff with a clinical background. We recently found out by accident that they made a change which allowed clients to see their progress notes through Onpatient without informing us. This is potentially very harmful to patients and the clinical relationship, and completely unethical. When we notified them their initial response was that we should follow their blog to stay informed of changes!! After several more email exchanges we received an apology but I have no confidence that they really take seriously the clinical implications d if something like this. We've had numerous issues with them over the years but this was the worst. If you're shopping for EHRs I'd look elsewhere before your who office is committed to a system

Stay away!!!. I gave DrChrono the benefit of the doubt for over a year, upgrading to this relatively expensive EHR due to the slick-looking iPad app and promise of regular support. Unfortunately, their system remains unstable to this day, with repeated outages, disconnects from the server, and with promised features never arriving.

When contacting support, you might as well save yourself some time and find a wall to bang your head against in frustration. Typically you’ll get no response whatsoever, but occasionally you’ll be upgraded to “Level 2” support, where a friendly email tells you to try restarting your device.

The final straw for me, though, was when we attempted to cancel the service and switch to a more reliable EHR. As one might guess from the “amazing” technical support, your cancellation requests will simply be ignored, and you cannot remove your credit card information from the system! They will continue to bill you, citing their “30 day cancellation policy” while failing to actually respond and cancel your account at any time. Eventually we had to simply open a dispute with the credit card company instead to get this shut off.

Find a functional EHR with functional staff, and stay away from this snake oil

Great EHR/Practice Management Software. As a new doctor, I started with the free version of drchrono, which was great as I was starting my practice. Great EHR. I'm updating a review from a while ago. Have now been using it for 18 mo. For anyone that tried this before, and perhaps felt it was not good enuf for them,  come back &  have a 2nd look. There has been a whirlwind of activity by the staff at DrC in the past 3 months, truly making this a class act. With an IPad Mini in my pocket for pt encounters using a much improved & faster app, an improved computer browser interface for all staff & myself to enter data, & now even the pt encounter note, a much more complete iPhone App, that allows me to access the complete pt chart when fielding a phone call, and another iPhone app in the works, that will make exchanging data from a pt, from 1 doc to another as easy as my paying for a cup of coffee at Starbuck's. This is & will remain the major player in iPad EHRs.

Have been using for online scheduling by our 2 offices x 1 year, then gradually increased my usage to EMR. This is a great company! Highly knowledgable, responsive staff. The EHR app (iPad) allows for easy customization of templates to use when seeing pts. The EMR app for phones allows you to see your schedule, log a phone call, and now since the recent upgrade I can access THE ENTIRE PATIENT record on my phone. I think that phone app, alone, makes this a unique system. Can also do various data entry from a browser so staff can enter meds, vital signs, problem list without needing an iPad for every employee.  And there is an iPad/kindle app for the patient, when they first come to  the office to fill out demographics, email address, etc. Soon the company is going to have an upgrade to allow patients to fill out this info online before their visit, which can be customized into an intake questionnaire, with the data syncing into the EMR. This company is wanting to become the best in the field, and they are moving fairly quickly in that direction

iPad Perfecto. I have been on the hunt for an app that will allow me the freedom to custom design  tools to meet my needs in clinical counseling.  This app exceeds my expectations!  I have created several templates tailor-made to the type of counseling I am engaged in: individual, couples, and/or family.  
The comprehensive platform allows me immediate access to whatever information I need or function I or my staff need to perform: clinical notes, insurance billing, etc...
Be prepared for the time you will need initially to learn the system and template design aspect.  However, their team is "Johnny on the spot" to assist you.  In addition, they have numerous video tutorials to walk you through the processes.
It lacks a 5-star due to the price packages they currently offer.  Some of the features I don't need come in the lower plans while a couple of the ones I could use are in the higher plans.  I hope they consider revamping the plans.  Also, it the program is designed with the medical model concept, including meaningful use objectives.  This does not apply to me professionally.  I would like to see a design for professionals in clinical counseling who follow best-practice objectives.
DrChrono is off to a great start.  With feedback from their users perhaps they can make the program more appealing from customized profession standpoint and fee packages that allow users to select the features they want

drchrono = best medical record app. Definitely the best medical record app on the market for doctors

Cardiology. Update: on a positive note Ellis & Harish are keeping us updated on all our billing requests and are working to get all our concerns resolved. Can't wait to see the updates!

The clinical component is great but the billing needs a lot of work. Our main issues has been with the billing portion. You can't really do any accurate audits if needed & it doesn't track which user leaves notes, schedules patient appointments or cancels them. It needs improvement, but #1 is the billing. They have a lot of work to do

Health Care CFO. Updated !!!  The problem with blue shield of ca has been fixed. 

Communication continues to be good. Harish and Ellis are very helpful. 

We are still working on item 2 below. The patient portal is in beta testing and should help with online payments and reporting upgrades are on the way too. 

I'm still giving 5 stars because I am confident my needs will be addressed and it is overall a great value for our practice 

We are a complex multi specialty medical and surgical group and Dr Chrono is working well for us so far 

With multiple entities and tax IDS and ancillary services (audiology and hearing devices) I was concerned that such a new software could not accommodate our many needs....

The medical records and charting functions are liked by my docs and the system is not so overly complex (as others are) that our long tenured medical records assistant is working with it well. 

The company is constantly adding functionality that makes sense in all aspects of the software. The billing functionality is much improved with a cpt and dx picklist on the iPad  and claims processing is fast. e eligibility ( checking for valid insurance info) is the best I have seen

Three things would get this software a 5 star rating 

1- connection to blue shield of California. Right now the system does not check eligibility or electronically bill this payer. boo. 

2- further improvement to the billing function - more functionality in patient statements , ERA posting by date, reconciliation of ERAs to the bank. ( just because they said they paid you, you still have to check! ), patient payments online 

3- improved communication and capacity. We have had some run time slowness and some instances of surprise upgrades that have thrown us sideways along the way, and some spotty response time with support. We should have been made aware of major changes and upgrades without having to search their blog. 

I have been informed by the dr chrono staff that all of these issues above are being addressed. And item three is much improved as of late. 

Overall this is great value for the money. The amount of money we are saving per year by switching systems and not paying for outside services for statements, patient reminders and transcription ( all included in the basic dr chrono minthly subscription)  is well into 5 figures. 

Anyone looking for a client reference or more information is welcome to tweet me at lawsonstacy33

So far, so great. I am a chiropractor and also prescribe yoga exercises and yoga therapy for my patients. Drchrono allows me to record and document effectively, and saves me time. Yes iPad based, good support, strong possibilities. I am a naturopathic physician, this program doesn't support what I do completely, I also perform manual therapy like massage, PT, and chiropractor so the program has a lot to do.  The cost is great, but over the career I would want to purchase or it is going to get expensive but for now this works. It works with square but square has more expensive rates than phone swipe.  There are many things in development so it's evolving but there are hiccups.  One is that on patient needs to be more friendly to multiple visits per month patients.  It's great to have them check the data each time but not have to sign consent forms each time. I always ask questions to the staff, sometimes they are helpful and sometimes not.  They always reply by email if issue was satisfactorily answered sometimes before I get the answer. Some of the support on phone is difficult to speak to because of strong foreign accents. This is difficult when I am in a hurry.  I have lost a good chunk of chart note at one point but this has only happened 1 time. (Make sure you click on space around note to autosave if doing note through browser). The software online has glitch they are working out that shows  auto fill even if I have not clicked on the field. (May have been fixed by now bc I did not see this today). Some of the icd-9s don't auto fill like I would think, I.e rib strain comes up as a problem with cancer of rib or nothing comes up.  Hope this gives you a little flavor of the program and support.  I will stick with it but there have been times where I did want to transfer to another naturopathic friendly program like charm or free program like practice fusion
Great app - must have!. An EMR unlike anything I have ever seen, a must for a doctor, try the onpatient check-in app with it!

Great EHR.... I'm a solo practitioner who recently began using drchrono to manage all ehr and medical billing needs for my practice. I've had a great experience. I find the software and the support/service quite robust, much moreso than my previous ehr and medical billing software and service, and am very happy I switched. On the software side, the web calendar implementation, the live claims feed, and the navigation and workflow in the iPad and iPhone apps stand out, and, on the support/service side, the easy availability of live training and support have been extremely helpful.

I have hit a couple snags getting set up with electronic billing, but as they turned out to be the fault of the payer and/or clearinghouse, I can't blame drchrono for those. Similarly, while there are a few software features I'd like to see implemented, they are far enough beyond what I've seen in other ehr and medical billing systems that I don't count it against drchrono that they haven't been implemented yet.

I highly recommend drchrono!

VERY happy!!!. I am VERY happy with my decision to switch to DrChrono! It was a little frustrating at first building my templates specific to my needs and getting them to work the way I wanted, but tech support was awesome and quick to help. Now that I have it just how I want it, it saves me a TON of time and allows me to leave work at work and focus on my family when I leave rather than inputting notes and financial information into my old system every evening. What used to take me 10-20 minutes to input now takes me less than a minute shaving hours off my work day. I also LOVE the "copy previous note" feature since I practice Acupuncture and many patients repeat the same treatment for several visits, this saves me a ton of time. If another patient comes in for the same issue, I can go to the other patients note and copy/paste their plan notes into the new patients chart and then tweak it to meet that patients needs.  The billing integration with the Apollo plan is nothing short of amazing. I can input my coding on the iPad and my office manager can figure out the patient's co-pay or coinsurance on her computer at the front desk and instantly send the bill to insurance while I get the patient's note completed and begin seeing the next patient. Everything is streamlined!  I <3 DrChrono and so does my family!!!!!

Working things break, problems never get solved. This is a constantly evolving iPad based EMR. Unfortunately, the developers seem to spend a lot of time making it look pretty and almost no time fixing serious problems. Accounts receivable is unusable. I haven't been able to send an accurate patient statement in the two years I have been using the platform. Medicare secondary payer billing has never worked - this is a serious flaw since federal law requires physicians to file Medicare claims electronically for their patients or face serious penalties. I have written off thousands of dollars of MSP claims waiting for this simple claim function to work. Although insurance claims are filed accurately through the clearinghouse, the data coming back is frequently (i.e. almost always) misapplied to the patient account. 

The actual medical record part is solid. Once you are used to and understand the work flow, a quality progress note which supports the level of billing is generated nicely. It would be equally nice to be able to actually get paid for the work done. 

If you decide to try this EMR, DO NOT switch your accounts receivable until they have resolved the myriad billing issues. I am having to restart a separate billing system and manually submit MSP claims as I have lost confidence in their interest in or ability to fix the claims and accounts receivable problems

It's a start. This is my 3rd EMR in 7 years. It has excellent potential. However, there are many things that are lacking. There need to be multiple templates for different disease conditions in such a way that scrolling through mobile forms is not necessary. Good potential. Best emr for charting the way I chart but needs a lot of front end customization.  I agree with other reviews that they need help with the billing side before I can use it, but I have high hopes for it.  As a chiropractor, I haven't liked many of the emr's on the market, would have to change a lot the way in which I chart.  Bugs I don't like about it:
1.  Can't print a range of dates- not very time saving to have to open each note when printing notes for attorneys.
2.  Does not have a tracking feature to watch pain scale drop over time.  
3.  No outcome tools tracking (Oswestry).
4.  Would be nice to have a calendar at a glance when you pull up their chart so you can visually see when they were in.  
5.  You can customize templates but would be nice if you could also set up a flowchart.  If pt has neuro sx, it would be nice to have that part of the template open up.  Found some of my templates getting too big, but still learning how to create them.
6.  Would like to be able to link fields to a PDF to be able to customize things like massage therapy referrals, imaging referrals.
7.  Lastly in my state we are required to have the Dr's address on the chart and dr chrono does not do this.  I will be bringing this up with their support team.

Best thing about it is that I am able to set up chart notes the way already chart and they look professional.  If their design team takes the billing side issues seriously, they could rake in a large market share since they are native to the iPad which lets face it ...rocks!

Has potential. This app makes it simple and even enjoyable to generate office notes, but that's about it. The practical features that most physicians need in an EHR such as document management, patient data tracking, medical device and lab interface, and intraoffice messaging are nonexistent or primitive at best. Lack of functionality of the companion Internet based application means that everyone involved with the patients care, including the receptionist, nurse, and medical assistant, will need an iPad to document any patient data beyond demographics, smoking status, and vital signs. Thats a lot of iPads! The functionality of the EMR is highly linked to the scheduler, which is highly annoying if you already use a different scheduler or practice management system. 

There are some cool features, like the ability to take and import patient photos on the spot, and the ability to mark up documents using your finger. While they add to the wow factor, I would much prefer the ability to order labs, document a phone call, or refill medications without all the work arounds.

I really wanted to like and use this product, especially since the customer support staff is so responsive. Unfortunately, no matter how hard I tried, I couldn't make it work for my practice. I have no doubt that in time, say maybe a year or so, the people at DrChrono will have a fully functioning EHR will blow the others out the water, but right now it isn't ready for prime time

Where it's Good it Excels, Where It's Not Good It's Horrible. Multiple modes for note creation on iPad focused interface: customizable templates, text macros, very accurate voice recognition / transcription are some of it's pluses.  Built-in FAX out capability directly from the application at no extra cost is a wonderful feature as well.
Most grave problem, however, is a very buggy e-prescribing interface that has recurrent connectivity issues with the app, especially with e-refills. An almost fatal design flaw is the puzzling lack of display of critical information about prescription written electronically. There is no display of the date of rx, dispense amount, number of refills, and destination pharmacy on the iPad app.  Although all of this information but the destination pharmacy can be viewed via a web portal, trying to use the iPad app to accurately manage refill requests is simply not possible at this point.
Other areas of concern include a complete absence of a usable intrapractice messaging from within the app and no availability to interface directly with commercial reference laboratories at this point.
This is an excellent clinical note writing tool, but lacks critical features needed to be a complete full-service EHR at this point.  The developer staff seem pretty responsive, so maybe in a couple of releases it will be a five star, comprehensive app

Worth every penny. I have never been excited enough to write a review for an app until now. My father and I are both chiropractors and we have been in search for a long time for an EHR program that made notation efficient but still gave us the flexibility and control we needed for our notes to contain our distinct personalities and flavor. Dr Chrono does just that. 

I believe what makes this app a must by and so unique is that for the first time in history you have real programmers approaching medical software from the view point that even medical apps can be elegant and simple. All other medical software has been purely utilitarian and about as colorful as concrete.

Another unique factor is that since Dr Chrono is an app, and for the first time in history our medical software updates no different than Angry Birds! We have been with DrChrono for long enough now to see the amazing leaps and bounds they add with each update. In the past these type of upgrades would only be accessible through expensive repurchasing each year. 

Finally and possibly most important to our love of DrChrono is our amazing rep Katelyn. What other company in the world gives personalized service 7 days a week and at all hours of the day and night? Both me and my father had to grow into our love of DrChrono. Initially we had some issues but to the amazing credit of our rep and DrChrono, they told us to itemize what we wanted and they not only have repeatedly accommodated all of our requests, they have exceeded our expectations and amazed us. 

Thank you DrChrono for aspiring to be something fun and innovative. Thank you for feeling like family and thank you for bringing our EHR nightmares to an end. I promise that if you buy DrChrono it will be the best decision you ever made for your business. 

- Dr John Giacalone Jr and Sr

App "trial" is useless. It's merely a sales-pitch ploy.. 1. Downloaded "Free App."
2. Try to use, but must enter personal info to "Create Free Account" including phone # and email address. <sigh>
3. I go ahead and enter the info, so I can use app already.
4. After submitting info, still can't use, but am told to look for a confirmation email. <sigh>
5. I check email for my log-in info. Nothing. <sigh>
6. I wait, and wait. ...and wait.
7. I wait some more.
8. The next day I receive a friendly email, responding to the "interest I indicated," conveying their hopes I'm enjoying my "trial."
9. Confused. Trial? I thought this version was free. Now it's a trial? Wait! I haven't even enjoyed any trial, even at that. All I've "enjoyed" was a shiny app icon and a log-in screen. Did I miss a different "confirmation email"?
10. I look through my email inbox. No, I didn't miss it there. How about my spam folder? Not there either. <sigh>
11. I re-read the supposed "follow-up email." Oh, I read that their "dual platform service allows you to use an iPad in the exam room while your staff runs off of our secure website and the high reliability and high availability of the cloud." Wait! Does this mean this "free app" doesn't work without the "other" part of this "dual platform service!? So it's like so many other EMRs that also have iPad and iPhone access? But what about all those 5 Star reviews from "patients" who saw their MD using this app and downloaded it to their own iPad and "were so impressed after tinkering with it"?
12. I review the reviews from the "current version" of the app. I'm again impressed there is nothing less than 5 Star reviews, including the "patient," someone who was "impressed with the concept," and a "Dr. Ellis"? That's the name of the sales rep from Dr. Chrono who emailed me with an offer for a sales call. No! It can't be! Can it? <argh>
13. I check my pulse.
14. I decide to look at reviews from "previous versions." Now I see 1 Star and 2 Star reviews. I don't find any average reviews. It seems to be an "all or nothing" trend. But apparently, their "current version" is simply "perfect". Only rave reviews?
15. I investigate the problems necessitating new versions of the app and a list of updates with each version. My investigation comes up cold. For some reason, they don't provide any such documentation, except for the most recent "version." That doesn't make sense.
16. I then review the dates and times of new "versions" of the apps and realize they are "released" almost immediately following any negative reviews. As a result, all such negative reviews are simply archived with a "blank slate" and, what's this? ...there is a flurry of idealized reviews, all 5 Stars, from a variety of persons who seem to use extremely similar wording, style, and jargon to those "golden reviews" of versions past.
17. I realize I've been "hoodwinked." <argh>
18. I write a tersely-worded reply to Ellis. I just about press "send" but think again, "Am I being too harsh? Perhaps he was too eager, but nonetheless sincere in his 'kindly' sales email? Maybe their registration process is backlogged and and I'm reading too much into it?" <sigh>
19. I again check my pulse and decide to wait.
20. I wait a week a hear nothing. No email providing confirmation of a "free account." Is it possible they called my office? Certainly, but my staff routinely dismisses multiple "sales pitch" calls. They have not received any "account confirmation" call.
21. I again check the iPad app store for any new medical apps with any potential. Many with potential, but most EMR apps require a huge investment in time (sales pitch and negotiation over cost and set-up of accounts and servers) and then, of course, the actual exchange of money. The other apps, however, do not advertise "free" or insinuate any such thing. Dr. Chrono is unique in being free and having 5-Stars, to boot.
22. I again review the reviews and find no new reviews. The "flurries of idealized reviews" only clustering in the hours and days of a "new release" that seems to serve no other purpose than to "reset the ratings" after a negative review.
23. I rewrite my email into this review.
24. I am about to press "submit."
25. I will later check back with the iPad app store as I anticipate, (soon after submitting this review), that Dr. Chrono will suddenly release a newer version still with the requisite flurry of idealized 5-Star reviews, (all sounding strangely familiar - yet likely a bit more unique this time), within only days and hours of the predicted "new version." <sigh>
26. Henceforth, I will view with a bit more skeptical eye other iPad/iPhone apps with such hot and cold reviews. Is this an inherent problem with Apple's app stores? Does this suggest poor oversight and little quality control or regulation of Apple's new frontier? Perhaps it's a bit of the "Wild West" all over again. I can't help but hear the voice of Emilio Estevez yelling his battle-cry: "REGULATORS!"
27. I check current prices of "snake oil" and thank my lucky stars that charlatans always wear black hats and greasy mustaches.
28. Returning to my iPad, I see the comforting green icon of "dr chrono" in its disarming Dax font, as if big brother is smiling down upon me. I am at peace

Cutting Edge EMR/PM !. I have been with the dr Chrono group since May 2010 after literally researching emr's for the past 7-8 yrs. The initial draw was the iPad native app and the web based format and the personal attention I recd from the sales rep who actually spent about 2 hrs with me on the phone answering all of my questions, one by one.

As an apple converter from pc's about 6 months ago I felt comfortable that the iPad was here to stay and it would be my tool/interface for interacting with my patients after trying many, many tablet pc's that were just too bulky and with poor battery life.

In any event the dr Chrono group is committed to being state of the art AND fulfilling the necessary requirements for government reimbursements for EMR/EHR incentives and to be certified to allow for the meaningful use of the EMR, as well as the incentives for e-rxing and pqri incentives!, which my practice is well on it's way to fulfilling.

The iPad interface interacts instantaneously in real time with the web based portal and other ipads that my staff use for the vitals, assessments, etc and I have nearly eliminated paper, and hope to do so soon as the implementation of e- rxing is now available , pdf uploading into the ipad patients chart is available, customization for specialties, self customizable macros, soap notes, ability to copy previous soap notes and modify as needed, and lab, imaging, and voice dictation and digital inking to come soon I'm told.

I am also using the practice mgmnt part of the software and after some problems with enrolling all of my insurance payers, things are now flowing well and I can now check all of my claims, analytics of the office, on one easy interface on the web on my MacBook pro , iPad, iPhone or any other device that connects to the web.

In looking back, as one might guess, choosing a relatively young start up company to handle my clinics that I have built up over the past 19 years was a difficult decision, but let's face it, there are risks in business and after all , all successful companies started out small at the beginning and gained their success thru attention to detail, hard work, knowledge of the field, commitment, and accessibility as well as a personal touch, and dr Chrono has shown me and my staff all of these!!

Lastly, the start up costs with the free iPad made the decision even easier as most of the other EMR/PM companies are charging 3-4 times the cost with additional recurring monthly costs that are exaggerated as well as additional costs for tech support, updates, etc,...dr Chrono does not.

If you are hedging on choosing an EMR/PM software program please look to the dr Chrono group and at least give them a call and put them to the test, compare and contrast with the other programs out there as I did, and then make your decision, you will not be sorry!

Dr Sellinger

Excellent app. Extremely easy user interface, invaluable with keeping things organized within my practice and saves a TON of headaches! Highly recommended!
BEST EMR !!!!. Simply the best EMR app I have used

Great app easy to setup and use. Why has it taken so long for a company like DrChrono to come along? They are very responsive to customer needs and they are great to work with. If I ask for a feature I get it and there philosophy is that every other user of there app gets it which means we every user benefit and no big customization bill
Best EMR. Best EMR and best interface around. Saved my practice money and great support team!

A must for physicians.. Best iPad EMR on the market. A must for any doctor, the SOAP notes and dictations are great!

Signup, you won't regret it!

Exactly what you want, easy to use, and awesome billing features!!!. This is eactly what I needed. It puts all the essential info right in front of me like SOAP notes, prescribing history, etc. and some of the relatively simple features like having the picture of the patient in the corner are amazingly helpful (hate to admit it, but managing 3 patient at a time, we've all picked up the wrong chart), now you never have to worry about it ebcause the patient's picture is right in front of you. This software's power is beyond the iPad as well. The iPad app works perfectly with the DrChrono front desk interface, so the superbill is electronically transfered to the admin immediate after I see the patient, and the software even cautions them against modifier overuse!! Would be difficult to go back to the old paper superbill now. I just bring my iPad between my two offices

Pretty but very rudimentary. In it's present form, this program is pretty but not very sophisticated. There are no templates and dictation is basically having the iPad create an audio file which is sent to transcription. May work for solo primary care practitioner. I am pretty sure it will not meet meaningful use criteria as there is no way of entering PMH, ROS, SH, FH and detailed exam; nor is there any linkage between the visit parameters and the CPT or Dx coding. Would recommend comparing with PracticeFusion and CareCloud (although I don't think either of these work on an iPad natively)
Excellent. This is definitely the best EMR on the market.  It has everything you need for a physican to hit the ground running!
It can only get better!!!!. Dr. Chrono EMR is great now and can only get better in the future.  Great start guys, I can't wait to see it improve.  EMRs are the future!!!

This is the future!. Dr. Chrono is amazing.  I feel like I am practicing medicine on the Starship Enterprise.  The Ipad app puts everything at my fingertips in an efficient manner that saves me time.  Easy to use and incorporate into your practice

Ronald Johnston. It is great to see DrChronos moving ahead of times by keeping the current technology interface friendly,
and for a good purpose.....the medical industry. Congratulations!!!

Fantastic!. This is a fantastic iPad app. I had been waiting for the iPad with a software like this all my for a long time. I have tried tablet pc's with some custom software, but never quite as functional as this one. The ease at which I am able to access patient information is fantastic. I am waiting for the version that has transcription built in. Also wondering if they are going to release a server for a locally hosted solution

Second, although I compose communications to the medical team in organized paragraph form, once sent, they appear as one long run-on paragraph. I downloaded it for the first time and encountered a bug. Couldn’t submit a required form before my appointment, it wasn’t recognizing that a required component was filled out. Tried clearing out and re entering all fields of the form multiple times and it was still broken. Contacted support to report the bug, they replied saying it must be user error and telling me to update everything. Everything was already up to date and I encountered this bug in the first hour of downloading the app from the App Store; if the support agent had taken five seconds to see that my account was created same day as my report, they could have recognized they were giving me bad advice. So I replied reiterating that was not the issue. They never even opened my reply; I know, because my email client has read receipts. That was a month ago. I managed to submit the form and unblock myself by refilling it out on their website, but most customers with less technical experience would not think to do this as a workaround.

Absolute garbage. Completely untested. Sends a link that redirects to the App Store to login with the same link that redirects to the App Store. Most places send you a link that opens the app. Then it says to enter the URL only they don’t want the URL because you have to remove any “unnecessary” elements like http: and ANY slashes… like the ones after the .com (or .md) in the URL. Of course NO PART of the sent URL works. You must enter the PORTAL’S DOMAIN not an URL, and it’s nothing like the App Store URL they sent! Oh, and they have the medical office send a check-in text DAYS ahead of time (reply 1,2 or 3… yes 3), followed by a check-in email that bypasses the app and goes to the website where you must face the most counter-intuitive calendar: why yes my birth date is today, or I don’t mind scrolling back decades and clicking through all the months! There’s a forgotten credentials link in case you forgot your name, birth date, and the very e-mail address you’re using, because there’s no passwords just 2FA to your phone. At the website: NO CHECK-IN for upcoming appointments! But now you have the portal domain to put into the app and… it’s the website. However, the annoying calendar now lets you scroll months if you want, but it selects a seemingly random day (today’s date based) that you have to click back onto the calendar to override. Spoiler alert: no check-in on the app version of the website! Only a millennial would believe it’s ok to just mirror the website in an app

Maybe when the app asks for the URL, they mean the code appended to the appointment invitation link for this app. Nope! You’ve just created an undefined error.

Ok. I get it. Maybe there’s some help somewhere. NOPE! Contact your provider! Oh, and despite the app being published by a company called “Modernizing Medicine,” the login page is copyright 2020 (5 years ago as of this writing.)

One last thing: if you try to contact your provider for help by replying to the text invite you got first sending you to this app, GOOD LUCK! You’ll be met with a “Default Help Message” response. (Really. That’s all it says. In its entirety. It’s kinda cute, actually, but completely useless.)

Horrible app, but I’m stuck with it. My doctors’ offices chose to use this “service”, not me. Each office requires a unique log-in, not just a user name and password, but a practice identifier, too. Once I’ve logged into one practice, changing to another is a laborious task of logging out, re-opening the app, having FaceID (which will not turn off, no matter how many times I flick the in-app toggle to do so) log me back into the practice I just logged out of, logging out again, closing the app, then hand entering the info for the three fields to log into the practice I needed to.

There’s no reason I shouldn’t be able to–like ever other medical records app–log into the app once, then switch practices in-app. At the very least, there should be a drop down of saved practice URLs at the login screen.

When you’re trying to add dependent children as patients, it’s basically impossible. This app should have the capability to add a family account, where 80% of patient familles have the same insurance, email, phone#, address, and family history. The second worst feature is every condition, surgery, and family history are listed using medical terminology. It took me over 2 hours to add one daughter, because I had to Google every medical term listed for accurate reporting. Now I’m having problems logging into the Dermatologist portal. The app is only using my ENT’s portal info to gain access. When I try logging in to the Dermatologist portal with the same email but different password, it tells me my login information is incorrect. There are many other problems with the app, however the two discussed are the most problematic. This Patient Portal program has been dreadfully designed and executed, having no value to a layperson

3/2026 Mobile EHR is now unusable for providers. Subject: Mobile EHR is currently unusable for providers

Message:

I run a medical practice and rely on mobile access. The current mobile experience is not functional:
	•	The original Charm EHR app appears deprecated with no replacement
	•	The browser version on iPhone does not render correctly (missing panels, scaling issues, unusable navigation)
	•	Splitting functionality into multiple apps (scheduler, etc.) does not replace full EHR access

Right now, it is not possible to reliably chart or manage patients from a phone.

This is a major workflow issue for clinicians running modern or concierge practices.

Request:
	•	A fully functional mobile EHR app OR
	•	A properly mobile-optimized browser experience

I would strongly encourage prioritizing this—mobile usability is currently a limiting factor in continuing to use Charm

I concur. The above review reflects my experience with this app. Dallas to know it’s not my own ineptitude, though I’m struggling with technology, more and more. But this app has driven me to absolute distraction. So frustrating. I’m getting older and slower soon to be left in the dust. My interactions with this app has been 100% negative

app not functional. annoying app trying to change password or logging in with otp by the time u leave the app to check the otp in the email goes back to the beginning of logging in and cant get pass that coz its in loops  so cant log in successfully
No way to delete your account. This app has all your private information and when you discontinue with a provider there is no way to delete it from Charm. That is a big privacy issue

App no longer working after update. This app is generally a bit more clunky than other medical chart apps, but after the update yesterday the app no longer loads. I am still able to use the web version, which not at all geared for phone use, so while I still have access to my practitioners, it is incredibly cumbersome. Please fix this!

Very well designed. A medical app that keeps it simple.  It gets straight to the point with the items you really need like appointments and messaging your provider.  I recommend the app to all that utilize charm health
Weight Loss. Love it! No side effects ! Loosing the weight !!

Charm Portal: A Time Capsule of Tech Regression. I was genuinely relieved to see Charm Portal had an app—because their mobile website is a nightmare. You have to pinch, zoom, and squint just to navigate basic functions. But the app? Somehow manages to offer even less than the clunky, outdated site. This is supposed to support communication with my naturopathic doctor, yet it feels like the developers haven’t looked at a single modern medical portal in the last two decades. The interface is primitive, the functionality is stripped down, and the entire experience is a frustrating exercise in wasted time. Charm Portal isn’t just behind—it’s archaeological. If you’re hoping for streamlined access to your provider, this app will test your patience and your screen-tapping endurance

Ok App. This app is ok but there are so many things that could be updated to make the user experience better. Like being able to add appointments to personal calendars easily. Also integrating information like consistency in date management and identity in questionaries

Poor security practice. Be aware if your password is compromised, changing it does not revoke access to anyone that is already logged in to your account. A password change should immediately log out all devices and require the new password to log back in, especially when sensitive medical information is involved
